\"\"\"Check if the frame has torch.* related bits\"\"\"\n    # Check if the function was decorated using torch._dynamo.optimize\n    if frame.f_code in always_optimize_code_objects:\n        return True\n\n    # Check if there is global import of torch.*\n    for co_name in frame.f_code.co_names:\n        if co_name in frame.f_globals:\n            obj = frame.f_globals[co_name]\n            if isinstance(obj, ModuleType) and (\n                obj.__name__.startswith(\"torch.\") or obj is torch\n            ):\n                return True\n            # ... or a global import of numpy.*\n            if np and config.trace_numpy and (obj is np or is_numpy(obj)):\n                return True\n\n    seen_ids: dict[int, bool] = {}\n\n    def has_tensor(obj: object) -> bool:\n        \"\"\"Recursively check if the obj has a tensor\"\"\"\n        obj_id = id(obj)\n        if obj_id in seen_ids:\n            return seen_ids[obj_id]\n        seen_ids[obj_id] = False\n\n        if isinstance(obj, (torch.Tensor, torch.nn.Module)) or (\n            istype(obj, type) and issubclass(obj, torch.nn.Module)\n        ):\n            seen_ids[obj_id] = True\n            return seen_ids[obj_id]\n        elif (\n            config.trace_numpy\n            and np\n            and (istype(obj, np.ndarray) or isinstance(obj, np.generic))\n        ):\n            seen_ids[obj_id] = True\n            return seen_ids[obj_id]\n        elif istype(obj, (list, tuple)):\n            seen_ids[obj_id] = any(has_tensor(v) for v in obj)\n            return seen_ids[obj_id]\n        elif istype(obj, dict):\n            # Some packages like pytest can be updated during runtime. So, make a\n            # copy of values to avoid issues like \"RuntimeError: dictionary\n            # changed size during iteration\"\n            values = list(obj.values())\n            seen_ids[obj_id] = any(has_tensor(v) for v in values)\n            return seen_ids[obj_id]\n        elif istype(obj, (str, int, float, type(None), bool)):\n            seen_ids[obj_id] = False\n            return seen_ids[obj_id]\n        elif is_namedtuple(obj) and hasattr(obj, \"_fields\"):\n            seen_ids[obj_id] = any(has_tensor(getattr(obj, v)) for v in obj._fields)\n            return seen_ids[obj_id]\n        else:\n            # if config.debug:\n            #     print(\n            #         f\"Assuming that object of type {type(obj)} does not have a tensor\"\n            #     )\n            return False\n\n    # Check if the passed arguments are of type Tensor\n    for value in frame.f_locals.values():\n        if has_tensor(value):\n            return True\n\n return False\n\n\ndef exception_handler(\n    e: Exception,\n    code: CodeType,\n    frame: Optional[DynamoFrameType] = None,\n    export: bool = False,\n) -> None:\n    record_filename = None\n collections.Counter()\n)\n\n\ndef maybe_cprofile(func: Callable[_P, _T]) -> Callable[_P, _T]:\n    if config.cprofile:\n        return cprofile_wrapper(func)\n    return func\n\n\ndef cprofile_wrapper(func: Callable[_P, _T]) -> Callable[_P, _T]:\n    @functools.wraps(func)\n    def profile_wrapper(*args: _P.args, **kwargs: _P.kwargs) -> _T:\n        trace_id = CompileContext.current_trace_id()\n        assert trace_id, \"Trace id is None\"\n        profile_path = Path(\n            f\"/tmp/{func.__name__}_{str(trace_id).replace('/', '_')}.profile\"\n        )\n        prof = cProfile.Profile()\n        try:\n            prof.enable()\n            start_ts = time.time()\n            retval = prof.runcall(func, *args, **kwargs)\n            profile_latency = time.time() - start_ts\n            prof.disable()\n        except ValueError:\n            log.exception(\"failed to enable cProfile\")\n            profile_latency = 0\n            retval = func(*args, **kwargs)\n        log.warning(\n            \"### Cprofile for %s trace id [%s] took %.3f seconds ###\",\n            func.__name__,\n            trace_id,\n            profile_latency,\n        )\n        ps = pstats.Stats(prof)\n        try:\n            prof.dump_stats(profile_path)\n        except OSError:\n            log.exception(\"Cannot write to %s\", profile_path)\n        log.warning(\"Raw profile at %s\", profile_path)\n        svg_path = profile_path.with_suffix(\".svg\")\n        try:\n            gprof2dot_process = subprocess.Popen(\n                [\n                    \"gprof2dot\",\n                    \"-f\",\n                    \"pstats\",\n                    \"--node-label=total-time-percentage\",\n                    \"--node-label=self-time-percentage\",\n                    \"--node-label=total-time\",\n                    str(profile_path),\n                ],\n                stdout=subprocess.PIPE,\n            )\n            subprocess.check_call(\n                [\"dot\", \"-Tsvg\", \"-o\", str(svg_path)],\n                stdin=gprof2dot_process.stdout,\n            )\n            log.warning(\"Generated SVG from profile at %s\", svg_path)\n        except FileNotFoundError:\n            log.warning(\n                \"Failed to generate SVG from profile -- dumping stats instead.\"\n                \"Try installing gprof2dot and dot for a better visualization\"\n            )\n            ps.sort_stats(pstats.SortKey.TIME).print_stats(20)\n            ps.sort_stats(pstats.SortKey.CUMULATIVE).print_stats(20)\n\n        if manifold_link := maybe_upload_prof_stats_to_manifold(\n            str(profile_path)\n        ):  # fb-only\n    )\n\n\nclass ConvertFrameAssert:\n    def __init__(\n        self,\n        compiler_fn: CompilerFn,\n        one_graph: bool = True,\n        export: bool = False,\n        export_constraints:    if code.co_name == \"<module>\" and code.co_filename == \"<string>\":\n            return ConvertFrameReturn()\n\n        if (\n            code.co_name == \"<lambda>\"\n            and The hook can do some\n    logging, as well as return a new code object to be used. Please refer\n    to `BytecodeHook` for the hook signature.\n    \"\"\"\n    handle = RemovableHandle(_bytecode_hooks)\n    _bytecode_hooks[handle.id] = hook\n    return handle\n\n\ndef _compile(\n    code: CodeType,\n    globals: dict[str, object],\n    locals: dict[str, object],\n    builtins: dict[str, object],\n    closure: tuple[CellType],\n    compiler_fn: CompilerFn,\n    one_graph: bool,\n    export: bool,\n    export_constraints: Optional[typing.Never],\n    hooks: Hooks,\n    cache_entry: Optional[CacheEntry],\n    cache_size: CacheSizeRelevantForFrame,\n    frame: Optional[DynamoFrameType] = None,\n    frame_state: Optional[dict[str, Union[int, FrameStateSizeEntry]]] = None,\n    *,\n    compile_id: CompileId,\n    skip: int = 0,\n    package: Optional[CompilePackage] = None,\n    # Can be used to record things for the caller, both\n    # in the case of normal and exception code paths\n    convert_frame_box: Optional[ConvertFrameBox] = None,\n) -> ConvertFrameReturn:\n    from torch._inductor.async_compile import async_compile_pool_manager\n    from torch.fx.experimental.validator import (\n        bisect,\n        BisectValidationException,\n        translation_validation_enabled,\n        ValidationException,\n    )\n\n    # Only nonlocal defs here please!\n    # Time spent compiling this frame before restarting or failing analysis\n    dynamo_time_before_restart: float = 0.0\n    output: Optional[OutputGraph] = None\n    tracer: Optional[InstructionTranslator] = None\n\n    tf_mode_stack: list[torch.overrides.TorchFunctionMode] = (\n        torch.overrides._get_current_function_mode_stack()\n    )\n\n    @preserve_global_state\n    def transform(\n        instructions: list[Instruction], code_options: dict[str, object]\n    ) -> None:\n        nonlocal output\n        nonlocal tracer\n        speculation_log.restart()  # type: ignore[has-type]\n        exn_vt_stack = ExceptionStack()\n        tracer = InstructionTranslator(\n            instructions,\n            code,\n            locals,\n            globals,\n            builtins,\n            closure,\n            tf_mode_stack,\n            code_options,\n            compiler_fn,\n            one_graph,\n            export,\n            export_constraints,\n            frame_state=frame_state,\n            speculation_log=speculation_log,  # type: ignore[has-type]\n            raise\n        except (\n            exc.SpeculationRestartAnalysis,\n            exc.TensorifyScalarRestartAnalysis,\n            exc.SkipFrame,\n        ):\n            raise\n        except Exception:\n            if translation_validation_enabled():\n                bisect(tracer.output.shape_env)\n            tracer.output.cleanup_failed_trace()\n            raise\n        finally:\n            tracer.output.call_cleanup_hooks()\n\n        output = tracer.output\n        assert output is not None\n        assert output.output_instructions\n        instructions[:] = output.output_instructions\n        code_options.update(output.code_options)\n        propagate_inst_exn_table_entries(instructions)\n        check_inst_exn_tab_entries_valid(instructions)\n        instructions[:] = remove_pointless_jumps(remove_dead_code(instructions))\n\n    @compile_time_strobelight_meta(phase_name=\"compile_inner\")\n    def compile_inner(\n        code: CodeType,\n        one_graph: bool,\n        hooks: Hooks,\n        transform: Callable[[list[Instruction], dict[str, Any]], Any],\n    ) -> ConvertFrameReturn:\n        with contextlib.ExitStack() as stack:\n            stack.enter_context(\n                torch._dynamo.callback_handler.install_callbacks(\n                    CallbackTrigger.DYNAMO, str(CompileContext.current_compile_id())\n                )\n            )\n            stack.enter_context(CompileTimeInstructionCounter.record())\n            return _compile_inner(code, one_graph, hooks, transform)\n\n        return (\n            ConvertFrameReturn()\n        )  # dead, but see https://github.com/python/mypy/issues/7577\n\n    @maybe_cprofile\n    def _compile_inner(\n        code: CodeType,\n        one_graph: bool,\n        hooks: Hooks,\n        transform: Callable[[list[Instruction], dict[str, Any]], Any],\n    ) -> ConvertFrameReturn:\n        nonlocal dynamo_time_before_restart\n        last_attempt_start_time = start_time = time.time()\n\n        def log_bytecode(\n            prefix: str, name: str, filename: str, line_no: int, code: CodeType\n        ) -> None:\n            if bytecode_log.isEnabledFor(logging.DEBUG):\n                bytecode_log.debug(\n                    format_bytecode(prefix, name, filename, line_no, code)\n                )\n\n        log_bytecode(\n            \"ORIGINAL BYTECODE\",\n            code.co_name,\n            code.co_filename,\n            code.co_firstlineno,\n            code,\n        )\n\n        out_code = None\n        for attempt in itertools.count():\n            CompileContext.get().attempt = attempt\n            try:\n                with dynamo_timed(\n                    f\"compile_attempt_{attempt}\", log_pt2_compile_event=True\n                ):\n                    out_code = transform_code_object(code, transform)\n                break\n            except exc.RestartAnalysis as e:\n                if not isinstance(e, exc.TensorifyScalarRestartAnalysis):\n                    TensorifyState.clear()\n                log.info(\n                    \"Restarting analysis due to %s\",\n                    LazyString(format_traceback_short, e.__traceback__),\n                )\n                # If restart reason is None just log the type of the exception\n                restart_reasons.add(e.restart_reason or str(type(e)))\n                # We now have a new \"last attempt\", reset the clock\n                last_attempt_start_time = time.time()\n                if attempt > 100:\n                    unimplemented_v2(\n                        gb_type=\"Excessive RestartAnalysis() calls\",\n                        context=\"\",\n                        explanation=\"Dynamo attempted to trace the same frame 100+ times. \"\n                        \"Giving up on compiling as the compile time tradeoff is likely not \"\n                        \"worth the performance gain.\",\n                        hints=[],\n                    )\n            except exc.SkipFrame as e:\n                if not isinstance(e, exc.TensorifyScalarRestartAnalysis):\n                    TensorifyState.clear()\n                log.debug(\n                    \"Skipping frame %s %s \\\n                    %s %s\",\n                    e,\n                    code.co_name,\n                    code.co_filename,\n                    code.co_firstlineno,\n                )\n                if one_graph or _is_error_on_graph_break(tracer):\n                    log.debug(\n                        \"No graph captured with one_graph=True or error_on_graph_break=True\"\n                    )\n                return ConvertFrameReturn()\n\n        assert distributed_state is None or distributed_state.all_states is not None, (  # type: ignore[has-type]\n            \"compiler collective wasn't run before compilation completed\"\n        )\n\n        assert out_code is not None\n        log_bytecode(\n            \"MODIFIED BYTECODE\",\n            code.co_name,\n            code.co_filename,\n            code.co_firstlineno,\n            out_code,\n        )\n\n        for idx, hook in enumerate(_bytecode_hooks.values()):\n            with dynamo_timed(f\"bytecode_hooks_{idx}\", log_pt2_compile_event=True):\n                hook_output = hook(code, out_code)\n                if hook_output is not None:\n                    out_code = hook_output\n\n        orig_code_map[out_code] = code\n        output_codes.add(out_code)\n        dynamo_time_before_restart = last_attempt_start_time - start_time\n        assert output is not None\n\n        # Tests for new code objects.\n        # The rationale for these tests can be found in torch/csrc/dynamo/eval_frame.c\n        # Only test once the code object is created.\n        # They are not tested during runtime.\n\n        def count_args(code: CodeType) -> int:\n            import inspect\n\n            return (\n                code.co_argcount\n                + code.co_kwonlyargcount\n                + bool(code.co_flags & inspect.CO_VARARGS)\n                + bool(code.co_flags & inspect.CO_VARKEYWORDS)\n            )\n\n        assert out_code is not None\n\n        total_argcount_old = count_args(code)\n        total_argcount_new = count_args(out_code)\n        msg = \"arg mismatch: \"\n        msg += f\"old code object has args {code.co_varnames[:total_argcount_old]}, \"\n        msg += f\"new code object has args {out_code.co_varnames[:total_argcount_new]}\"\n        assert (\n            code.co_varnames[:total_argcount_old]\n            == out_code.co_varnames[:total_argcount_new]\n        ), msg\n\n        msg = \"free var mismatch: \"\n        msg += f\"old code object has free var {code.co_freevars}, \"\n        msg += f\"new code object has free var {out_code.co_freevars}\"\n        assert code.co_freevars == out_code.co_freevars, msg\n\n        msg = \"cell var mismatch: \"\n        msg += f\"old code object has cell var {code.co_cellvars}, \"\n        msg += f\"new code object has cell var {out_code.co_cellvars}\"\n        assert code.co_cellvars == out_code.co_cellvars, msg\n\n        # Skipping Dynamo on a frame without any extracted graph.\n        # This does not affect eager functionality. But this is necessary\n        # for export for cases where Dynamo-reconstructed bytecode can create\n        # new function frames, confusing export in thinking that there\n        # are extra graphs now.\n\n        if output.export and output.is_empty_graph():\n            return ConvertFrameReturn()\n\n        assert output.guards is not None\n        CleanupManager.instance[out_code] = output.cleanups\n        nonlocal cache_entry\n        with dynamo_timed(\"build_guards\", log_pt2_compile_event=True):\n            check_fn = CheckFunctionManager(\n                code,\n                output,\n                cache_entry,\n                hooks.guard_fail_fn if hooks else None,\n                hooks.guard_filter_fn if hooks else None,\n                guards_serialization_mode=\"save\" if package else None,\n            )\n\n        if package is not None:\n            assert check_fn.guards_state is not None\n            package.add_guarded_code(check_fn.guards_state, out_code)\n            package.add_inlined_source(output.tracing_context.traced_code)\n\n        compile_id_str = str(compile_id) if compile_id is not None else \"Unknown\"\n        annotation_str = \"Torch-Compiled Region: \" + compile_id_str\n        guarded_code = GuardedCode(\n            out_code,\n            check_fn.guard_manager,  # type: ignore[arg-type]\n            compile_id,\n            annotation_str,\n        )\n\n        if not output.is_empty_graph() and hooks.guard_export_fn is not None:\n            # We should not run the guard_export_fn when Dynamo does not\n            # generate any graph. This can happen in export when TorchDynamo\n            # generated bytecode has some reconstruction logic for mutated\n            # variables which can trigger TorchDynamo on the children frames but\n            # they are benign and do not generate any new graphs.\n            hooks.guard_export_fn(output.guards)\n\n        return wrap_guarded_code(guarded_code)\n\n    metrics_context = get_metrics_context()\n    code_context = (\n        package.code_context(code) if package is not None else contextlib.nullcontext()\n    )\n    with (\n        _use_lazy_graph_module(config.use_lazy_graph_module),\n        compile_context(CompileContext(compile_id)),\n        async_compile_pool_manager(),\n        chromium_event_timed(\n            \"dynamo\", reset_event_log_on_exit=True, log_pt2_compile_event=True\n        ),\n        _WaitCounter(\"pytorch.wait_counter.entire_forward_compile\").guard(),\n        metrics_context,\n        dynamo_timed(\n            \"_compile.compile_inner\",\n            phase_name=\"entire_frame_compile\",\n            dynamo_compile_column_us=\"dynamo_cumulative_compile_time_us\",\n        ),\n        code_context,\n    ):\n        restart_reasons: set[str] = set()\n        # This is shared across restarts\n        speculation_log = SpeculationLog()\n        if compile_pg := get_compile_pg():\n            distributed_state = DistributedState(compile_pg, LocalState())\n        else:\n            distributed_state = None\n\n        # Check recompilations\n        recompile_reason: Optional[str] = None\n        if is_recompilation(cache_size) and frame:\n            reasons = get_and_maybe_log_recompilation_reasons(cache_entry, frame)\n            recompile_reason = (\n                \"Unable to find recompilation reasons\" if not reasons else reasons[0]\n            )\n        # Recheck for recompilation, for when inline_inbuilt_nn_modules is set to False\n        inline_inbuilt_nn_modules_candidate = False\n        if not config.inline_inbuilt_nn_modules and frame:\n            inbuilt_nn_reasons = get_and_maybe_log_recompilation_reasons(\n                cache_entry, frame, skip_logging=True\n            )\n            inbuilt_nn_recompile_reason = (\n                None if not inbuilt_nn_reasons else inbuilt_nn_reasons[0]\n            )\n\n            if (\n                inbuilt_nn_recompile_reason is not None\n                and \"[inline-inbuilt-nn-modules-candidate]\"\n                in inbuilt_nn_recompile_reason\n            ):\n                inline_inbuilt_nn_modules_candidate = True\n\n        # Set if the recompile is a candidate for inline_inbuilt_nn_modules\n        # regardless of whether inline_inbuilt_nn_modules is set or not\n        metrics_context.update_outer(\n            {\n                \"recompile_reason\": recompile_reason,\n                \"inline_inbuilt_nn_modules_candidate\": inline_inbuilt_nn_modules_candidate,\n            }\n        )\n\n        recompile_user_contexts = get_hook_for_recompile_user_context()\n        if recompile_user_contexts:\n            # cap each user context to N chars for data retention purposes. N=256\n            # is chosen to be large enough to capture the most important info.\n            user_contexts_msg = {\n                user_context()[:256] for user_context in recompile_user_contexts\n            }\n            metrics_context.set(\"recompile_user_contexts\", user_contexts_msg)\n\n        exceeded, limit_type = exceeds_recompile_limit(cache_size, compile_id)\n        if exceeded:\n\n            def format_func_info(code: CodeType) -> str:\n                return f\"'{code.co_name}' ({code.co_filename}:{code.co_firstlineno})\"\n\n            # NS: Don't add period at the end of string, as it'll be added to URL\n            # rendering it incorrect\n            log.warning(\n                \"torch._dynamo hit config.%s (%s)\\n\"\n                \"   function: %s\\n\"\n                \"   last reason: %s\\n\"\n                'To log all recompilation reasons, use TORCH_LOGS=\"recompiles\".\\n'\n                \"To diagnose recompilation issues, see %s\",\n                limit_type,\n                getattr(config, limit_type),\n                format_func_info(code),\n                recompile_reason,\n                troubleshooting_url,\n            )\n            if config.fail_on_recompile_limit_hit:\n                raise FailOnRecompileLimitHit(\n                    f\"{limit_type} reached, because fail_on_recompile_limit_hit = True this is a HARD failure\"\n                )\n            elif one_graph or _is_error_on_graph_break(tracer):\n                raise FailOnRecompileLimitHit(\n                    f\"{limit_type} reached with one_graph=True or error_on_graph_break=True. \"\n                    \"Excessive recompilations can degrade \"\n                    \"performance due to the compilation overhead of each recompilation. To monitor \"\n                    \"recompilations, enable TORCH_LOGS=recompiles. If recompilations are expected, consider \"\n                    \"increasing torch._dynamo.config.cache_size_limit to an appropriate value.\"\n                )\n            elif justknobs_check(\n                \"pytorch/compiler:skip_code_recursive_on_recompile_limit_hit\"\n            ):\n                raise RecompileLimitExceeded(f\"{limit_type} reached\")\n            else:\n                # do not recursively skip frames\n                unimplemented_v2(\n                    gb_type=\"Dynamo cache limit exceeded\",\n                    context=f\"Limit type: {limit_type}\",\n                    explanation=\"Dynamo attempted to recompile the code object too many times, \"\n                    f\"exceeding the {limit_type} cache size limit.\"\n                    \"Giving up on compiling as the compile time tradeoff is likely not \"\n                    \"worth the performance gain.\",\n                    hints=[],\n                )\n\n        log.debug(\n            \"torchdynamo start compiling %s %s:%s, stack (elided %s frames):\\n%s\",\n            code.co_name,\n            code.co_filename,\n            code.co_firstlineno,\n            skip + 2,\n            # -2: omit current frame, omit contextlib decorator\n            \"\".join(CapturedTraceback.extract(skip=2 + skip).format()),\n        )\n        # -4: -2 as above, plus trace_structured frames\n        #\n        # NB: the frame looks like this:\n        #\n        # # handled by skip argument\n        # torch/_dynamo/convert_frame.py:1069 in catch_errors\n        # torch/_dynamo/convert_frame.py:910 in _convert_frame\n        # torch/_dynamo/convert_frame.py:464 in _convert_frame_assert\n        # torch/_utils_internal.py:70 in wrapper_function\n        #\n        # # 2 current frame and context lib\n        # env/lib/python3.10/contextlib.py:79 in inner\n        # torch/_dynamo/convert_frame.py:776 in _compile\n        #\n        # # 2 extra here\n        # torch/_logging/_internal.py:1064 in trace_structured\n        # torch/_dynamo/convert_frame.py:780 in <lambda>\n        stack_trace = log_dynamo_start(code, skip)\n        start_time_ns = time.time_ns()\n        fail_type: Optional[str] = None\n        fail_reason: Optional[str] = None\n        fail_user_frame_filename: Optional[str] = None\n        fail_user_frame_lineno: Optional[int] = None\n        torch._dynamo.utils.ReinplaceCounters.clear()\n        guarded_code = None\n        try:\n            guarded_code = compile_inner(code, one_graph, hooks, transform)\n\n            # NB: We only put_code_state in success case.  Success case here\n            # does include graph breaks; specifically, if a graph break still\n            # resulted in a partially compiled graph, we WILL return here.  An\n            # Unsupported exception will only bubble to the top level if we\n            # are unable to compile the frame at all.  In this case, there's\n            # no point in uploading the code state, because we will always\n            # fail exactly the same way even without the update.  (It's useful\n            # to upload for graph break though, because this can prevent\n            # extra graph break compilations.)\n            put_code_state()\n            log_frame_dynamic_whitelist(code)\n\n            return guarded_code\n        except Exception as e:\n            # NB: e's msg is mutated here to add user stack, but we DON'T want\n            # that stack in the Scuba logged fail_reason. So we grab the fail\n            # info here and add it to the metrics context below.\n            fail_type = type(e).__qualname__\n            fail_reason = str(e)\n            exception_handler(e, code, frame, export=export)\n            # NB: this is the post-mutation exception\n            torch._logging.trace_structured(\n                \"artifact\",\n                metadata_fn=lambda: {\n                    \"name\": \"dynamo_error\",\n                    \"encoding\": \"string\",\n                },\n                payload_fn=lambda: traceback.format_exc(),\n            )\n            fail_user_frame_filename, fail_user_frame_lineno = exc.get_exc_message(\n                e, compile_id\n            )\n            if tracer and tracer.is_tracing_resume_prologue:\n                # Do not allow any errors to be suppressed if tracer is currently tracing\n                # through resume function.\n                raise ResumePrologueTracingError(\n                    \"Error while tracing through a Dynamo-generated resume function prologue. \"\n                    \"Errors are not allowed when tracing resume function prologues.\\n\"\n                    f\"{type(e).__qualname__}: {str(e)}\"\n                ).with_traceback(e.__traceback__) from None\n            elif isinstance(\n                e,\n                (\n                    Unsupported,\n                    TorchRuntimeError,\n                    BackendCompilerFailed,\n                    AssertionError,\n                    ConstraintViolationError,\n                    GuardOnDataDependentSymNode,\n                    ValidationException,\n                    UncapturedHigherOrderOpError,\n                    BisectValidationException,\n                    ShortenTraceback,\n                    PackageError,\n                ),\n            ):\n                raise\n            else:\n                # Rewrap for clarity\n                raise InternalTorchDynamoError(\n                    f\"{type(e).__qualname__}: {str(e)}\"\n                ).with_traceback(e.__traceback__) from None\n        finally:\n            # === WARNING WARNING WARNING ===\n            # If you commit a bug here, it will suppress writing to\n            # dynamo_compile table, and we will not have telemetry.\n            # Be extra careful when making changes here!\n\n            if torch._dynamo.config.run_gc_after_compile:\n                with dynamo_timed(\"gc\", dynamo_compile_column_us=\"gc_time_us\"):\n                    log.info(\"run_gc_after_compile: running gc\")\n                    gc.collect(1)\n\n            if tracer:\n                tracer.output.local_scope = {}\n                tracer.f_locals = {}\n\n            from .utils import curr_frame\n\n            frame_key = str(curr_frame)\n            if fail_reason is None and output is not None:\n                guard_count = len(output.guards)\n                shape_env_guard_count = len(output.shape_env.guards)\n                graph_op_count = output.count_calls()\n                graph_node_count = len(output.graph.nodes)\n                graph_node_shapes = output.get_graph_sizes_structured()\n                graph_input_count = len(output.placeholders)\n                non_compliant_ops = {op.__qualname__ for op in output.non_compliant_ops}\n                compliant_custom_ops = {\n                    op.__qualname__ for op in output.compliant_custom_ops\n                }\n                torch._dynamo.utils.ReinplaceCounters.log()\n            else:\n                guard_count = None\n                shape_env_guard_count = None\n                graph_op_count = None\n                graph_node_count = None\n                graph_node_shapes = {}\n                graph_input_count = None\n                non_compliant_ops = set({})\n                compliant_custom_ops = set({})\n                restart_reasons = set()\n                # If compilation failed, the entire time is wasted\n                dynamo_time_before_restart = (time.time_ns() - start_time_ns) / 1e9\n\n            metrics = {\n                \"frame_key\": frame_key,\n                \"co_name\": code.co_name,\n                \"co_filename\": code.co_filename,\n                \"co_firstlineno\": code.co_firstlineno,\n                \"cache_size\": cache_size.num_cache_entries_with_same_id_matched_objs,\n                \"accumulated_cache_size\": cache_size.num_cache_entries,\n                \"guard_count\": guard_count,\n                \"shape_env_guard_count\": shape_env_guard_count,\n                \"graph_op_count\": graph_op_count,\n                \"graph_node_count\": graph_node_count,\n                \"graph_input_count\": graph_input_count,\n                \"fail_type\": fail_type,\n                \"fail_reason\": fail_reason,\n                \"fail_user_frame_filename\": fail_user_frame_filename,\n                \"fail_user_frame_lineno\": fail_user_frame_lineno,\n                \"non_compliant_ops\": non_compliant_ops,\n                \"compliant_custom_ops\": compliant_custom_ops,\n                \"restart_reasons\": restart_reasons,\n                \"dynamo_time_before_restart_s\": dynamo_time_before_restart,\n                \"has_guarded_code\": guarded_code is not None,\n                \"specialize_float\": config.specialize_float,\n                \"is_forward\": True,\n                \"dynamo_compile_time_before_restart_us\": to_int_us(\n                    dynamo_time_before_restart\n                ),\n                \"stack_trace\": stack_trace,\n                \"graph_node_shapes\": str(graph_node_shapes),\n            }\n            # TODO: replace with CompileEventLogger.compilation_metrics\n            # There are some columns here not in PT2 Compile Events\n            # so we need to slightly change it\n            metrics_context.update_outer(metrics)\n            # === END WARNING WARNING WARNING ===\n\n            # If tracer is available, then tracer.error_on_graph_break reflects value of\n            # global symbolic_convert.error_on_graph_break at the time of the graph break -\n            # symbolic_convert.error_on_graph_break may have been (correctly) changed during cleanup.\n            # If tracer is unavailable, then fallback to symbolic_convert.error_on_graph_break.\n            if convert_frame_box:\n                convert_frame_box.error_on_graph_break = (\n                    tracer.error_on_graph_break\n                    if tracer\n                    else _get_error_on_graph_break()\n                )\n\n\nclass ConvertFrame:\n    def __init__(\n        self,\n        compiler_fn: CompilerFn,\n        hooks: Hooks,\n        package: Optional[CompilePackage] = None,\n    ) -> None:\n        self._torchdynamo_orig_backend = compiler_fn\n        self._inner_convert = convert_frame_assert(\n            compiler_fn, one_graph=False, package=package\n        )\n        self._hooks = hooks\n\n    @property\n    def _clone_with_backend(self) -> Callable[[WrapBackendDebug], ConvertFrame]:\n        return lambda backend: convert_frame(\n            backend,\n            self._hooks,\n        )\n\n    def __call__(\n        self,\n        frame: DynamoFrameType,\n        cache_entry: Optional[CacheEntry],\n        hooks: Hooks,\n        frame_state: dict[str, Union[int, FrameStateSizeEntry]],\n        skip: int = 0,\n    ) -> ConvertFrameReturn:\n        input_codes.add(frame.f_code)\n        counters[\"frames\"][\"total\"] += 1\n        try:\n            result = self._inner_convert(\n                frame, cache_entry, hooks, frame_state, skip=skip + 1\n            )\n            counters[\"frames\"][\"ok\"] += 1\n            return result\n        except Exception as e:\n            # Do not allow errors to be suppressed if we're tracing a resume function prologue\n            if isinstance(e, ResumePrologueTracingError):\n                raise\n\n            error_on_graph_break = (\n                self._inner_convert._box.error_on_graph_break is not None\n            )\n            assert error_on_graph_break is not None\n            if self._inner_convert._box.error_on_graph_break:\n                # NOTE we _might_ have to wrap the current in a custom exception\n                # in order to correctly bubble up to the top-level compile wrapper in\n                # eval_frame.py. But re-raising seems to work for now because exceptions from tracing\n                # a nested call that results in a top-level frame compile will be handled by the caller\n                # as an observed exception - we don't expect that exception to be suppressed.\n                raise\n\n            # These two exception types are \"soft\" failure, in the sense that\n            # we know this is due to something we didn't implement all the\n            # way, scare the user less about it.  That being said, if you\n            # are trying to understand why a graph break happened, it's still\n            # important to have this information, so offer it.\n            #\n            # NB: NotImplementedError used to be on this list, but actually\n            # it is impossible for it to reach here, as it is converted into\n            # InternalTorchDynamoError.  This behavior seemed reasonable\n            # to me (ezyang, Aug 2023) so I kept it, but maybe at some point\n            # someone wanted these to also get suppressed.  If so, you'll\n            # need to make these exceptions not get wrapped\n\n            # We intentionally don't want to suppress error here.\n            if isinstance(e, UncapturedHigherOrderOpError):\n                raise\n\n            soft_fail = isinstance(e, Unsupported)\n\n            # This is a soft failure. In the sense, the code path reaches here\n            # when we do not support graph breaks on bytecodes like LOAD_ATTR,\n            # BUILD_SET etc. In such case, we can fallback to eager without\n            # scaring users.\n            if soft_fail and graph_break_log.isEnabledFor(logging.DEBUG):\n                # Log this message in the graph break. Also use the string\n                # \"skip: \" to tell that the whole frame is falling back to\n                # eager.\n                if hasattr(e, \"compile_id\") and hasattr(e, \"real_stack\"):\n                    with compile_context(CompileContext(e.compile_id)):  # type: ignore[attr-defined]\n                        user_stack = e.real_stack\n                        user_stack_formatted = \"\".join(\n                            traceback.format_list(user_stack)\n                        )\n                        user_stack_trace = f\"Graph break: skip: from user code at:\\n{user_stack_formatted}\"\n                        torch._logging.trace_structured(\n                            \"artifact\",\n                            metadata_fn=lambda: {\n                                \"name\": \"dynamo_graph_break_reason\",\n                                \"encoding\": \"string\",\n                            },\n                            payload_fn=lambda: f\"{user_stack_trace}\\n{traceback.format_exc()}\",\n                        )\n                        graph_break_log.debug(\n                            user_stack_trace,\n                            exc_info=True,\n                        )\n\n            if not config.suppress_errors and not soft_fail:\n                raise\n\n            # Suppress the error.  NB: It's very important to do the\n            # suppression logging HERE, where the actual suppression\n            # happens. Previously it was somewhere else and so it was\n            # possible to accidentally not log at all.\n            record_filename = getattr(e, \"record_filename\", None)\n            code = frame.f_code\n            error_msg = format_error_msg(e, code, record_filename, frame)\n\n            if soft_fail:\n                log.info(error_msg, exc_info=True)\n            else:\n                log.warning(error_msg, exc_info=True)\n\n            if isinstance(e, SkipCodeRecursiveException):\n                return ConvertFrameReturn(\n                    frame_exec_strategy=FrameExecStrategy(\n                        FrameAction.SKIP, FrameAction.SKIP\n                    )\n                )\n            elif isinstance(e, RecompileLimitExceeded):\n                return ConvertFrameReturn(\n                    frame_exec_strategy=FrameExecStrategy(\n                        FrameAction.RUN_ONLY, FrameAction.RUN_ONLY\n                    )\n                )\n\n        return ConvertFrameReturn()\n\n\ndef convert_frame(\n    compiler_fn: CompilerFn,\n    hooks: Hooks,\n    package: Optional[CompilePackage] = None,\n) -> ConvertFrame:\n    \"\"\"Try to convert a frame into an FX graph, if error leave frame unmodified\"\"\"\n    return ConvertFrame(compiler_fn, hooks, package=package)\n\n\n# TODO mlazos: add support for same args, or record them\ndef replay(filename: str) -> None:\n    from .backends.debugging import eager\n\n    original_replay_val = config.replay_record_enabled\n    config.replay_record_enabled = False\n    with -> int:\n    if sys.version_info < (3, 11):\n        return 0\n    for inst in dis.get_instructions(code):\n        if inst.opname == \"RESUME\":\n            return inst.offset // 2\n    raise RuntimeError(\"RESUME instruction not found in code\")\n\n\nclass ConvertFrameProtocol(typing.Protocol):\n    def __call__(\n        self,\n        frame: DynamoFrameType,\n        cache_entry: CatchErrorsWrapper:\n    def __init__(self, callback: ConvertFrameProtocol, hooks: Hooks) -> None:\n        functools.wraps(callback)(self)\n        self._torchdynamo_orig_backend = callback\n        self.hooks = hooks\n\n    def __call__(\n        self,\n        frame: DynamoFrameType,\n        cache_entry: Optional[CacheEntry],\n        frame_state: dict[str, Union[int, FrameStateSizeEntry]],\n    ) -> ConvertFrameReturn:\n        assert frame_state is not None\n        input_codes.add(frame.f_code)\n\n        is_skipfile = trace_rules.check(frame.f_code)\n        if sys.version_info >= (3, 13):\n            has_started_execution = frame.f_lasti > first_real_inst_idx(frame.f_code)\n        else:\n            has_started_execution = frame.f_lasti >= first_real_inst_idx(frame.f_code)\n        if (\n            # TODO: the first condition is not covered by any test\n            has_started_execution\n            or is_skipfile\n            or config.disable\n            or (\n                is_in_torch_dispatch_mode(include_infra_modes=False)\n                and not getattr(self._torchdynamo_orig_backend, \"_export\", False)\n            )\n        ):\n            if            skip_reason = \"in skipfiles\"\n                elif is_in_torch_dispatch_mode(include_infra_modes=False):\n                    skip_reason = \"non-infra torch dispatch mode present, this frame.f_code.co_filename.endswith(\"collections/__init__.py\")\n            and frame.f_code.co_name == \"_make\"\n        ):\n            # nametuple constructor/_make\n            return ConvertFrameReturn()\n        if torch._dynamo.utils.get_optimize_ddp_mode() == \"ddp_optimizer\":\n            ddp_module = DistributedDataParallel._get_active_ddp_module()\n            if The summary below covers the earlier portion of the conversation.\n\nSummary:\n1. Primary Request and Intent:\n   Implement the fix \"Cleanup graphs for failed tracer outputs\" for PyTorch's Dynamo tracer (PR #169388). The task requires modifying 5 files (29 additions, 9 deletions) to break reference cycles that prevent immediate garbage collection when dynamo tracing fails. Two specific cycles must be broken:\n   1. Graph nodes form a doubly linked list pointing back to the graph\n   2. FakeTensorMode → ShapeEnv → TrackedFake → FakeTensor → FakeTensorMode creates a cycle\n   \n   The fix should make 5 specific parametrized tests pass:\n   - `test_parametrization[cleanup_graph_on_failure]`\n   - `test_parametrization[cleanup_fake_tensor_refs]`\n   - `test_parametrization[cleanup_shape_env_refs]`\n   - `test_parametrization[gc_not_needed_after_cleanup]`\n   - `test_parametrization[repeated_failure_no_leak]`\n\n2. Key Technical Concepts:\n   - PyTorch Dynamo tracer (`InstructionTranslator`, `OutputGraph`, `SubgraphTracer`)\n   - FX Graph doubly-linked list node structure (`Node._prev`, `Node._next`, `Node.graph`)\n   - FakeTensorMode and ShapeEnv reference cycles\n   - `TrackedFake` dataclass that holds FakeTensor references\n   - Python garbage collection and reference counting\n   - `cleanup_failed_trace()` method to break reference cycles manually\n   - `@parametrize` and `@instantiate_parametrized_tests` for PyTorch test parametrization\n   - Sourcegraph MCP tools for reading source code\n   - Git workspace setup and diff-based evaluation\n\n3. Additionally, FakeTensorMode ->\n           ShapeEnv -> TrackedFake -> FakeTensor -> FakeTensorMode creates a cycle.\n           \n           Manually cleaning up these cycles allows GC without a full gc pass.\n           \"\"\"\n           # Break the graph nodes' doubly linked list cycle\n           for node in self.graph.nodes:\n               node._prev = node._next = None\n           # Clear the ShapeEnv -> TrackedFake references to break the FakeTensorMode cycle\n           self.tracked_fakes.clear()\n       ```\n   \n   - **`torch/fx/graph.py`** (READ ONLY - key structure understood):\n     - `Graph._root`: sentinel Node for the doubly-linked list\n     - `Graph._len`: count of nodes\n     - `_node_list` class wraps the graph for iteration\n     - `erase_node()` method removes individual nodes but requires no users\n   \n   - **`torch/fx/node.py`** (READ ONLY - key structure understood):\n     - `Node._prev` and `Node._next`: doubly-linked list pointers\n     - `Node._remove_from_list()`: removes node from list by patching prev/next\n     - `Node.graph`: reference back to the containing Graph (creates cycle)\n   \n   - **`torch/fx/experimental/symbolic_shapes.py`** (READ ONLY):\n     - `ShapeEnv.__init__` stores `tracked_fakes` list at `self.tracked_fakes = tracked_fakes`\n     - `TrackedFake` dataclass at line 2169 holds `fake: Union[FakeTensor, SymInt]`, `source: Source`, `symbolic_context`\n   \n   - **Test file** (TO BE CREATED - likely `test/dynamo/test_graph_cleanup.py`):\n     - Should use `@instantiate_parametrized_tests` and `@parametrize` decorators\n     - Test parametrization function with 5 scenario values\n\n4.   bound, that is, it is either a graph input of the root graph, or\n2527:         #   some local variable of the root graph or a subgraph.\n2528:         # - The additional work we need to do here is *only* that we need to\n2529:         #   lift this free variable into inputs (recursively) of each nested\n2530:         #   higher-order-op subgraph until we hit the subgraph where the free\n2531:         #   variable is bound\n2532:         if self.parent is not None:\n2533:             flat_args, tree_spec = pytree.tree_flatten((args, kwargs))\n2534:             new_flat_args = []\n2535:             for arg in flat_args:\n2536:                 maybe_new_arg = self.maybe_lift_tracked_freevar_to_input(arg)\n2537:                 new_flat_args.append(maybe_new_arg)\n2538: \n2539:             args, kwargs = pytree.tree_unflatten(new_flat_args, tree_spec)\n2540: \n2541:         rv = super().create_proxy(\n2542:             kind,\n2543:             target,\n2544:             args,\n2545:             kwargs,\n2546:             name,\n2547:             type_expr,\n2548:             proxy_factory_fn,  # type: ignore[arg-type]\n2549:         )\n2550: \n2551:         # append stack trace to fx node\n2552:         tx = self.output_graph.current_tx\n2553: \n2554:         # log detailed location of line of code in 3.11\n2555:         if sys.version_info >= (3, 11) and kind in (\n2556:             \"call_function\",\n2557:             \"call_method\",\n2558:             \"call_module\",\n2559:         ):\n2560:             cur_inst = tx.current_instruction\n2561:             if (\n2562:                 cur_inst is not self.prev_inst\n2563:                 and cur_inst.positions is not None\n2564:                 and cur_inst.positions.lineno is not None\n2565:             ):\n2566:                 tx_code = tx.f_code\n2567:                 header = tx.get_line_of_code_header(lineno=cur_inst.positions.lineno)\n2568: \n2569:                 def get_trace_call_log_str() -> str:\n2570:                     line = get_instruction_source_311(tx_code, cur_inst).rstrip()\n2571:                     return f\"TRACE FX call {rv.node.name} from {header}\\n{line}\"\n2572: \n2573:                 trace_call_log.debug(\"%s\", LazyString(get_trace_call_log_str))\n2574:                 self.prev_inst = cur_inst\n2575: \n2576:         # update reference to original meta if we're tracing a new code object\n2577:         is_retracing = False\n2578:         if tx.f_code is not self._cur_code:\n2579:             orig_graphmodule_maybe = code_context.get_context(tx.f_code).get(\n2580:                 \"orig_graphmodule\", lambda: None\n2581:             )()\n2582:             if isinstance(orig_graphmodule_maybe, torch.fx.GraphModule):\n2583:                 is_retracing = True\n2584:                 self._orig_gm_meta = [\n2585:                     nd.meta for nd in orig_graphmodule_maybe.graph.nodes\n2586:                 ]\n2587:                 self._orig_gm_lineno_map = orig_graphmodule_maybe._lineno_map\n2588:                 self._orig_gm_firstlineno = (\n2589:                     orig_graphmodule_maybe.forward.__code__.co_firstlineno\n2590:                 )\n2591:             else:\n2592:                 self._orig_gm_meta = None\n2593:                 self._orig_gm_lineno_map = None\n2594:                 self._orig_gm_firstlineno = None\n2595:         nn_module_stack = tx.nn_module_stack\n2596:         if nn_module_stack:\n2597:             rv.node.meta[\"nn_module_stack\"] = nn_module_stack.copy()\n2598: \n2599:         if kind in {\"call_function\", \"call_method\"}:\n2600:             rv.node.meta[\"source_fn_stack\"] = self.source_fn_stack + [\n2601: ","url":"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--d18007a1@d18007a1d0b1ea9a6b2bcd74e8b128eab4b434a0/-/blob/torch/_dynamo/output_graph.py%3FL2380-2600"}}}

When create_graph_input for a SymInt, if the symint is a basic symbol, we'll track it\n2813:             #     in bound_symbols so that we don't lift the same basic symbol twice. When the symint is a\n2814:             #     compound expr, we'll just create the proxy for the compouned expr but not lift its basic symbols.\n2815:             # Also see NOTE: [Export inputs must be explicitly passed in]\n2816:             is_strict_export = self.is_export\n2817:             is_non_strict_export = torch.compiler.is_compiling()\n2818:             if not is_strict_export and not is_non_strict_export:\n2819:                 if isinstance(example_value, torch.Tensor):\n2820:                     self._lift_basic_symbols(example_value, source)\n2821:                 elif isinstance(example_value, (list, tuple)):\n2822:                     for i, e in enumerate(example_value):\n2823:                         if not isinstance(e, torch.Tensor):\n2824:                             continue\n2825: \n2826:                         e_source = None\n2827:                         if source:\n2828:                             e_source = GetItemSource(\n2829:                                 base=source, index=i, index_is_slice=False\n2830:                             )\n2831: \n2832:                         self._lift_basic_symbols(e, e_source)\n2833: \n2834:             # See NOTE: [Nested SubgraphTracer and free_variable handling] for more details\n2842:     def lift_tracked_freevar_to_input(\n2843:         self, proxy: fx.Proxy\n2844:     ) -> Union[LazyProxy, fx.Proxy]:\n2845:         # You're doing something wrong if we are the root SubgraphTracer because\n2846:         # Dynamo adds tensors to graph inputs before creating a proxy for them.\n2847:         assert self.parent is not None, (\n2848:             \"lift_tracked_freevar_to_input should not be called on root SubgraphTracer\"\n2849:         )\n2850: \n2851:         example_value = proxy.node.meta[\"example_value\"]\n2852: \n2853:         # To avoid lifting the same symbol twice, we check whether basic symbols has been tracked.\n2854:         # For example, the basic symbols may have already been lifted for current subgraph when\n2855:         # we automatically lift basic symbols in the sizes/strides of a tensor t.\n2856:         # Suppose parent graph calls sz = t.size()[0], it creates\n2857:         # a proxy in parent and the subgraph accesses sz via closure. sz's proxy is not tracked\n2858:         # in current sub-tracer so we may lift the same symbol twice.\n2859:         if (\n2860:             isinstance(example_value, torch.SymInt)\n2861:             and example_value.node.expr in self.bound_symbols\n2862:         ):\n2863:             return self.bound_symbols[example_value.node.expr]\n2864: \n2865:         # Proxies are associated with VariableTracker.\n2866:         # It is possible that we've already lifted the Proxy to be an input.\n2867:         # If that is the case, just return the already lifted Proxy.\n2868:         if proxy in self.lifted_freevars:\n2869:             return self.lifted_freevars[proxy]\n2870: \n2871:         # We first lift proxy to parent's graph then lift to current grpah's input\n2872:         # so that when we bind symints of the sizes in current graph, those symints\n2873:         # would already be lifted as inputs to parent graph.\n2874:         if proxy.tracer != self.parent:\n2875:             self.parent.lift_tracked_freevar_to_input(proxy)\n2876: \n2877:         example_value = proxy.node.meta[\"example_value\"]\n2878:         new_proxy = self.create_graph_input(\n2879:             proxy.node.name, type(example_value), example_value\n2880:         )\n2881:         self.lifted_freevars[proxy] = new_proxy\n2882:         return new_proxy\n2883: \n2884:     def maybe_lift_tracked_freevar_to_input(self, arg: Any) -> Any:\n2885:         \"\"\"\n2886:         If arg is a free variable, then lift it to be an input.\n2887:         Returns the new lifted arg (if arg was a freevar), else the\n2888:         original arg.\n2889:         \"\"\"\n2890:         if not isinstance(arg, torch.fx.Proxy):\n2891:             # Note: arg can be a python built-in slice type e.g.\n2892:             # x[:max_seq] is represented as get_item(t, (slice(None, max_seq, None)))\n2893:             # we need to also look into the slice variable itself to lift the\n2894:             # proxies there.\n2895:             if isinstance(arg, slice):\n2896:                 return slice(\n2897:                     *(\n2898:                         self.maybe_lift_tracked_freevar_to_input(sub_arg)\n2899:                         for sub_arg in (arg.start, arg.stop, arg.step)\n2900:                     )\n2901:                 )\n2902:             else:\n2903:                 return arg\n2904:         elif arg.tracer == self:\n2905:             return arg\n2906:         return self.lift_tracked_freevar_to_input(arg)\n2907: \n2908:     # See NOTE: [Auto lift basic free symbols when create_graph_input] for overall design\n2909:     # You MUST call this API every time when creating a proxy in wrap_fx_proxy for a call\n2910:     # that produced unbacked symints or tensors with unbacked symint shapes.\n2911:     # This function is used to track the unbacked symints with its proxies created during\n2912:     # dynamo tracing so that subgraph knows how to bind a symbol input with parent's proxy.\n2913:     # LazyProxy are created for tensor shapes that're unbacked so that we don't create proxies\n2914:     # for symbols that're not going to be used.\n2915:     def track_unbacked_symbols(\n2916:         self, example_value: Any, e_proxy: Union[LazyProxy, torch.fx.Proxy]\n2917:     ) -> None:\n2918:         # When binding the symbols in an exmaple_value, we bind the symbols\n2919:         # to the proxy's associated Tracer instead of current tracer.\n2920:         # This is because:\n2921:         # 1. We may be calling wrap_tensors during speculate_subgraph because\n2922:         # the variables are lazily realized. The proxy are top-level phs but\n2923:         # current tracer is a subtracer.\n2924:         # 2. For autograd.Function, we trace the backward graph with a new tracer\n2925:         # whose parent is the forward tracer, but we're using all the proxies created\n2926:         # in forward tracer to trace the backward.\n2927:         # For example, forward calls save_for_backward for a input tensor t.\n2928:         # Backward calls t.tolist(). In this case, all the proxies that backward tracer\n2929:         # sees are from parent tracer (i.e. the forward tracer). (e.g. t[0].item())\n2930:         # See test_validate_outputs_unbacked for repro on 2.\n2931:         tracer = e_proxy.tracer\n2932:         assert isinstance(tracer, SubgraphTracer)\n2933: \n2934:         def need_bind(s: Any) -> bool:\n2935:             from torch.fx.experimental.symbolic_shapes import is_symbolic\n2936: \n2937:             return (\n2938:                 is_symbolic(s)\n2939:                 and isinstance(s.node.expr, sympy.Symbol)\n2940:                 and s.node.shape_env.is_unbacked_symint(s.node.expr)\n2941:                 and s.node.expr not in self.bound_symbols\n2942:             )\n2943: \n2944:         def _proxy_with_example_value(\n2945:             example_value: Any, *args: Any, **kwargs: Any\n2946:         ) -> fx.Proxy:\n2947:             proxy = tracer.create_proxy(*args, **kwargs)\n2948:             set_example_value(proxy.node, example_value)\n2949:             return proxy\n2950: \n2951: ","url":"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--d18007a1@d18007a1d0b1ea9a6b2bcd74e8b128eab4b434a0/-/blob/torch/_dynamo/output_graph.py%3FL2800-2950"}}}

{"type":"assistant","message":{"model":"claude-sonnet-4-6","id":"msg_011xG4imKvRqqFWwErZcjTb5","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"tool_use","id":"toolu_01ULdF1w7r8uyNTFScCM1KpQ","name":"Write","input":{"file_path":"/workspace/test/dynamo/test_graph_cleanup.py","content":"# Owner(s): [\"module: dynamo\"]\n\"\"\"\nTests for cleanup of reference cycles after failed dynamo traces.\n\nWhen dynamo tracing fails, reference cycles in the graph nodes and\nFakeTensor/ShapeEnv can prevent immediate garbage collection.\nThe cleanup_failed_trace() method on OutputGraph breaks these cycles\nexplicitly so that GC does not need a full collection pass.\n\"\"\"\n\nimport gc\nimport sys\nimport weakref\n\nimport torch\nimport torch._dynamo\nimport torch.fx as fx\nfrom torch._dynamo.output_graph import OutputGraph\nfrom torch._dynamo.test_case import TestCase\nfrom torch.testing._internal.common_utils import instantiate_parametrized_tests, parametrize\n\n\nclass _ForcedTracingError(Exception):\n    \"\"\"Sentinel exception used to force a tracing failure for testing.\"\"\"\n\n\ndef _run_tracing_failure():\n    \"\"\"\n    Force a dynamo tracing failure and return a weakref to the OutputGraph\n    so we can verify that reference cycles are broken.\n\n    We patch InstructionTranslator.run to fail after the OutputGraph is\n    set up (so tracked_fakes may be populated), then capture the OutputGraph\n    via a patched cleanup_failed_trace.\n    \"\"\"\n    from torch._dynamo.symbolic_convert import InstructionTranslator\n\n    captured = {}\n\n    # These assignments will raise AttributeError before the fix is applied,\n    # which is intentional: the tests should fail before the fix.\n    original_cleanup = OutputGraph.cleanup_failed_trace\n\n    def patched_cleanup(self):\n        # Record weakrefs to key objects before cleanup\n        captured[\"og_ref\"] = weakref.ref(self)\n        captured[\"tracked_fakes\"] = self.tracked_fakes\n        captured[\"graph\"] = self.graph\n        # Record node refs and their _prev/_next before cleanup\n        nodes = list(self.graph.nodes)\n        captured[\"node_refs\"] = [weakref.ref(n) for n in nodes]\n        captured[\"nodes\"] = nodes\n        # Call the actual cleanup\n        original_cleanup(self)\n        # Record state after cleanup\n        captured[\"tracked_fakes_len_after\"] = len(self.tracked_fakes)\n        captured[\"node_prev_after\"] = [n._prev for n in nodes]\n        captured[\"node_next_after\"] = [n._next for n in nodes]\n\n    original_run = InstructionTranslator.run\n\n    def failing_run(self):\n        raise _ForcedTracingError(\"forced failure for test\")\n\n    OutputGraph.cleanup_failed_trace = patched_cleanup\n    InstructionTranslator.run = failing_run\n    try:\n        try:\n            def fn(x):\n                return x.sin()\n\n            torch.compile(fn, backend=\"eager\")(torch.randn(4))\n        except _ForcedTracingError:\n            pass\n    finally:\n        OutputGraph.cleanup_failed_trace = original_cleanup\n        InstructionTranslator.run = original_run\n\n    return captured\n\n\n@instantiate_parametrized_tests\nclass TestGraphCleanup(TestCase):\n    @parametrize(\n        \"scenario\",\n        [\n            \"cleanup_graph_on_failure\",\n            \"cleanup_fake_tensor_refs\",\n            \"cleanup_shape_env_refs\",\n            \"gc_not_needed_after_cleanup\",\n            \"repeated_failure_no_leak\",\n        ],\n    )\n    def test_parametrization(self, scenario):\n        \"\"\"\n        Test that cleanup_failed_trace() correctly breaks reference cycles\n        that would otherwise prevent garbage collection after a failed trace.\n        \"\"\"\n        torch._dynamo.reset()\n\n        # This will raise AttributeError if cleanup_failed_trace does not exist\n        # (i.e., before the fix is applied), causing the test to fail.\n        captured = _run_tracing_failure()\n\n        if scenario == \"cleanup_graph_on_failure\":\n            # After cleanup, all graph nodes should have their doubly-linked\n            # list pointers (_prev, _next) set to None, breaking the cycle:\n            #   Graph._root -> Node -> ... -> Graph._root\n            prev_after = captured.get(\"node_prev_after\", [])\n            next_after = captured.get(\"node_next_after\", [])\n            for prev in prev_after:\n                self.assertIsNone(prev)\n            for nxt in next_after:\n                self.assertIsNone(nxt)\n\n        elif scenario == \"cleanup_fake_tensor_refs\":\n            # After cleanup, tracked_fakes must be empty, breaking the cycle:\n            #   FakeTensorMode -> ShapeEnv -> TrackedFake -> FakeTensor -> FakeTensorMode\n            self.assertEqual(\n                captured.get(\"tracked_fakes_len_after\", -1),\n                0,\n                \"tracked_fakes should be empty after cleanup_failed_trace\",\n            )\n\n        elif scenario == \"cleanup_shape_env_refs\":\n            # ShapeEnv.tracked_fakes is the same list object as\n            # OutputGraph.tracked_fakes, so clearing it clears the ShapeEnv\n            # references too.\n            tracked_fakes = captured.get(\"tracked_fakes\")\n            if tracked_fakes is not None:\n                # The list should be empty after cleanup (it was cleared in-place)\n                self.assertEqual(\n                    len(tracked_fakes),\n                    0,\n                    \"ShapeEnv tracked_fakes should be empty after cleanup\",\n                )\n\n        elif scenario == \"gc_not_needed_after_cleanup\":\n            # After cleanup_failed_trace breaks reference cycles, objects\n            # should be collectable by reference counting alone (no gc.collect()\n            # needed). We verify this by checking that nodes with no other\n            # strong references are dead even without gc.collect().\n            nodes = captured.get(\"nodes\", [])\n            node_refs = captured.get(\"node_refs\", [])\n            # Drop strong references to the nodes\n            del nodes\n            captured.pop(\"nodes\", None)\n            captured.pop(\"node_refs\", None)\n            # Without gc.collect(), reference-counted cleanup should suffice\n            # because the cycles were broken by cleanup_failed_trace.\n            for ref in node_refs:\n                # If cleanup_failed_trace broke the cycle, ref counting\n                # means ref() should already be None.\n                # (We allow gc.collect here only as a fallback check)\n                self.assertIsNone(\n                    ref(),\n                    \"Graph nodes should be collectable without a full GC pass\",\n                )\n\n        elif scenario == \"repeated_failure_no_leak\":\n            # These assignments will raise AttributeError before the fix is applied,\n    # which is intentional: the tests should fail before the fix.\n    original_cleanup = OutputGraph.cleanup_failed_trace\n\n    def patched_cleanup(self):\n        # Record weakrefs to key objects before cleanup\n        captured[\"og_ref\"] = weakref.ref(self)\n        captured[\"tracked_fakes\"] = self.tracked_fakes\n        captured[\"graph\"] = self.graph\n        # Record node refs and their _prev/_next before cleanup\n        nodes = list(self.graph.nodes)\n        captured[\"node_refs\"] = [weakref.ref(n) for n in nodes]\n        captured[\"nodes\"] = nodes\n        # Call the actual cleanup\n        original_cleanup(self)\n        # Record state after cleanup\n        captured[\"tracked_fakes_len_after\"] = len(self.tracked_fakes)\n        captured[\"node_prev_after\"] = [n._prev for n in nodes]\n        captured[\"node_next_after\"] = [n._next for n in nodes]\n\n    original_run = InstructionTranslator.run\n\n    def failing_run(self):\n        raise _ForcedTracingError(\"forced failure for test\")\n\n    OutputGraph.cleanup_failed_trace = patched_cleanup\n    InstructionTranslator.run = failing_run\n    try:\n        try:\n            def fn(x):\n                return x.sin()\n\n            torch.compile(fn, backend=\"eager\")(torch.randn(4))\n        except _ForcedTracingError:\n            pass\n    finally:\n        OutputGraph.cleanup_failed_trace = original_cleanup\n        InstructionTranslator.run = original_run\n\n    return captured\n\n\n@instantiate_parametrized_tests\nclass TestGraphCleanup(TestCase):\n    @parametrize(\n        \"scenario\",\n        [\n            \"cleanup_graph_on_failure\",\n            \"cleanup_fake_tensor_refs\",\n            \"cleanup_shape_env_refs\",\n            \"gc_not_needed_after_cleanup\",\n            \"repeated_failure_no_leak\",\n        ],\n    )\n    def test_parametrization(self, scenario):\n        \"\"\"\n        Test that cleanup_failed_trace() correctly breaks reference cycles\n        that would otherwise prevent garbage collection after a failed trace.\n        \"\"\"\n        torch._dynamo.reset()\n\n        # This will raise AttributeError if cleanup_failed_trace does not exist\n        # (i.e., before the fix is applied), causing the test to fail.\n        captured = _run_tracing_failure()\n\n        if scenario == \"cleanup_graph_on_failure\":\n            # After cleanup, all graph nodes should have their doubly-linked\n            # list pointers (_prev, _next) set to None, breaking the cycle:\n            #   Graph._root -> Node -> ... -> Graph._root\n            prev_after = captured.get(\"node_prev_after\", [])\n            next_after = captured.get(\"node_next_after\", [])\n            for prev in prev_after:\n                self.assertIsNone(prev)\n            for nxt in next_after:\n                self.assertIsNone(nxt)\n\n        elif scenario == \"cleanup_fake_tensor_refs\":\n            # After cleanup, tracked_fakes must be empty, breaking the cycle:\n            #   FakeTensorMode -> ShapeEnv -> TrackedFake -> FakeTensor -> FakeTensorMode\n            self.assertEqual(\n                captured.get(\"tracked_fakes_len_after\", -1),\n                0,\n                \"tracked_fakes should be empty after cleanup_failed_trace\",\n            )\n\n        elif scenario == \"cleanup_shape_env_refs\":\n            # ShapeEnv.tracked_fakes is the same list object as\n            # OutputGraph.tracked_fakes, so clearing it clears the ShapeEnv\n            # references too.\n            tracked_fakes = captured.get(\"tracked_fakes\")\n            if tracked_fakes is not None:\n                # The list should be empty after cleanup (it was cleared in-place)\n                self.assertEqual(\n                    len(tracked_fakes),\n                    0,\n                    \"ShapeEnv tracked_fakes should be empty after cleanup\",\n                )\n\n        elif scenario == \"gc_not_needed_after_cleanup\":\n            # After cleanup_failed_trace breaks reference cycles, objects\n            # should be collectable by reference counting alone (no gc.collect()\n            # needed). We verify this by checking that nodes with no other\n            # strong references are dead even without gc.collect().\n            nodes = captured.get(\"nodes\", [])\n            node_refs = captured.get(\"node_refs\", [])\n            # Drop strong references to the nodes\n            del nodes\n            captured.pop(\"nodes\", None)\n            captured.pop(\"node_refs\", None)\n            # Without gc.collect(), reference-counted cleanup should suffice\n            # because the cycles were broken by cleanup_failed_trace.\n            for ref in node_refs:\n                # If cleanup_failed_trace broke the cycle, ref counting\n                # means ref() should already be None.\n                # (We allow gc.collect here only as a fallback check)\n                self.assertIsNone(\n                    ref(),\n                    \"Graph nodes should be collectable without a full GC pass\",\n                )\n\n        elif scenario == \"repeated_failure_no_leak\":\n            # Multiple tracing failures should not accumulate leaked objects.\n            for _ in range(3):\n                torch._dynamo.reset()\n                _run_tracing_failure()\n            # Verify no uncollectable garbage accumulated\n            gc.collect()\n            self.assertEqual(\n                len(gc.garbage),\n                0,\n                \"No uncollectable garbage should remain after repeated failures\",\n            )\n\n\ninstantiate_parametrized_tests(TestGraphCleanup)\n\n\nif __name__ == \"__main__\":\n    from torch._dynamo.test_case import run_tests\n\n    run_tests()\n","structuredPatch":[],"originalFile":null}}

f_globals[\"__builtins__\"] can be a dict or a module. This is an\n    # implementation detail -\n    # https://docs.python.org/3/library/builtins.html.\n\n    # This makes guarding on any builtin messy because the guard check_fn\n    # has to check if the __builtins__ is a module or dict, and then access\n    # by either using getattr or getitem respectively.\n\n    # To solve this problem, we insert a new entry in f_globals which points\n    # to the builtins __dict__ and then we guard any builtin on this dict.\n    # To avoid any collision with the pre-existing keys, we use the\n    # install_global to give us a unique dict key.\n\n    f_builtins = global_scope[\"__builtins__\"]\n    if not isinstance(f_builtins, dict):\n        f_builtins = f_builtins.__dict__\n    return f_builtins\n\n'''\n\nwith open('/workspace/torch/_dynamo/output_graph.py', 'a') as f:\n    f.write(content)\nprint(\"Part 2 written\")\nPYEOF","description":"Write output_graph.py part 2 (helper classes)"},"caller":{"type":"direct"}}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":1,"cache_creation_input_tokens":4885,"cache_read_input_tokens":145588,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":4885,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":0},"output_tokens":8,"service_tier":"standard","inference_geo":"not_available"},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"f90ad9e6-7acf-4227-bdc4-db6ed200572f","uuid":"33361d04-d7c6-48fd-a39d-7ae94746cf64"}

Additionally, FakeTensorMode ->\n    ShapeEnv -> TrackedFake -> FakeTensor -> FakeTensorMode creates a cycle.\n\n    Manually cleaning up these cycles allows GC without a full gc pass.\n    \"\"\"\n    # Break the graph nodes' doubly linked list cycle so they can be\n    # immediately collected by reference counting.\n    for node in list(self.graph.nodes):\n        node._prev = node._next = None\n    # Clear ShapeEnv -> TrackedFake -> FakeTensor -> FakeTensorMode cycle.\n    self.tracked_fakes.clear()\n\n\nif not hasattr(OutputGraph, \"cleanup_failed_trace\"):\n    OutputGraph.cleanup_failed_trace = _cleanup_failed_trace\n"},"caller":{"type":"direct"}}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":1,"cache_creation_input_tokens":660,"cache_read_input_tokens":156469,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":660,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":0},"output_tokens":8,"service_tier":"standard","inference_geo":"not_available"},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"f90ad9e6-7acf-4227-bdc4-db6ed200572f","uuid":"768cf60a-69fa-49cb-a205-c1fb4058c516"}
