{"type":"assistant","message":{"model":"claude-sonnet-4-6","id":"msg_015Tb1nD4sopYxyk7nNvWpFk","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"tool_use","id":"toolu_01JAaVVykjoERKkGZzvRurAP","name":"Write","input":{"file_path":"/workspace/test/dynamo/test_graph_cleanup.py","content":"# Owner(s): [\"module: dynamo\"]\n\"\"\"\nTests for cleanup of reference cycles after failed dynamo traces.\n\nWhen dynamo tracing fails, reference cycles in the graph nodes and\nFakeTensor/ShapeEnv can prevent immediate garbage collection.\nThe cleanup_failed_trace() method on OutputGraph breaks these cycles\nexplicitly so that GC does not need a full collection pass.\n\nThe following tests validate the cleanup behavior:\n- cleanup_graph_on_failure: graph node doubly-linked-list cycle is broken\n- cleanup_fake_tensor_refs: FakeTensorMode cycle is broken via tracked_fakes\n- cleanup_shape_env_refs: ShapeEnv->TrackedFake references are cleared\n- gc_not_needed_after_cleanup: broken cycles allow immediate GC w/o gc.collect\n- repeated_failure_no_leak: no accumulation of leaked objects across failures\n\"\"\"\n\nimport gc\nimport weakref\n\nimport torch\nimport torch._dynamo\nfrom torch._dynamo.output_graph import OutputGraph\nfrom torch._dynamo.test_case import TestCase\nfrom torch.testing._internal.common_utils import instantiate_parametrized_tests, parametrize\n\n\nclass _ForcedTracingError(Exception):\n    \"\"\"Sentinel exception used to force a tracing failure for testing.\"\"\"\n\n\ndef _run_tracing_failure():\n    \"\"\"\n    Force a dynamo tracing failure and return captured state from\n    the OutputGraph at the time cleanup_failed_trace() is called.\n\n    This function:\n    1. Patches OutputGraph.cleanup_failed_trace to capture pre/post state.\n    2. Patches InstructionTranslator.run to raise a forced exception.\n    3. Calls torch.compile to trigger the failure path in convert_frame.py.\n\n    If convert_frame.py has been fixed to call cleanup_failed_trace() in its\n    except-Exception block, the patched method will be called and `captured`\n    will be populated.  If convert_frame.py has NOT been fixed, `captured`\n    will remain empty.\n\n    Returns the `captured` dict (possibly empty if the fix is absent).\n    \"\"\"\n    from torch._dynamo.symbolic_convert import InstructionTranslator\n\n    captured: dict = {}\n\n    original_cleanup = OutputGraph.cleanup_failed_trace\n\n    def patched_cleanup(self):\n        # Record a weakref to the output graph itself.\n        captured[\"og_ref\"] = weakref.ref(self)\n\n        # Snapshot graph nodes before cleanup.\n        nodes = list(self.graph.nodes)\n        captured[\"nodes\"] = nodes\n        captured[\"node_refs\"] = [weakref.ref(n) for n in nodes]\n\n        # Snapshot tracked_fakes list (same object as ShapeEnv.tracked_fakes).\n        captured[\"tracked_fakes\"] = self.tracked_fakes\n\n        # Call the real implementation.\n        original_cleanup(self)\n\n        # Record post-cleanup state.\n        captured[\"tracked_fakes_len_after\"] = len(self.tracked_fakes)\n        captured[\"node_prev_after\"] = [n._prev for n in nodes]\n        captured[\"node_next_after\"] = [n._next for n in nodes]\n\n    original_run = InstructionTranslator.run\n\n    def failing_run(self):\n        raise _ForcedTracingError(\"forced tracing failure for test\")\n\n    # Apply patches.\n    OutputGraph.cleanup_failed_trace = patched_cleanup\n    InstructionTranslator.run = failing_run\n    try:\n        def fn(x):\n            return x.sin()\n\n        try:\n            torch.compile(fn, backend=\"eager\")(torch.randn(4))\n        except _ForcedTracingError:\n            pass\n    finally:\n        OutputGraph.cleanup_failed_trace = original_cleanup\n        InstructionTranslator.run = original_run\n\n    return captured\n\n\n@instantiate_parametrized_tests\nclass TestGraphCleanup(TestCase):\n    @parametrize(\n        \"scenario\",\n        [\n            \"cleanup_graph_on_failure\",\n            \"cleanup_fake_tensor_refs\",\n            \"cleanup_shape_env_refs\",\n            \"gc_not_needed_after_cleanup\",\n            \"repeated_failure_no_leak\",\n        ],\n    )\n    def test_parametrization(self, scenario):\n        \"\"\"\n        Tests that cleanup_failed_trace() is called during a tracing failure\n        and correctly breaks reference cycles.\n\n        These tests fail when:\n        - The fix to convert_frame.py (calling cleanup_failed_trace in the\n          except-Exception block) has not been applied, OR\n        - The cleanup_failed_trace method does not perform the expected cleanup.\n        \"\"\"\n        torch._dynamo.reset()\n\n        captured = _run_tracing_failure()\n\n        # Primary assertion: cleanup_failed_trace MUST have been called.\n        # Before the fix, convert_frame.py does not call cleanup_failed_trace()\n        # in its except-Exception block, so `captured` will be empty and this\n        # assertion will fail.\n        self.assertIn(\n            \"og_ref\",\n            captured,\n            \"cleanup_failed_trace() was not called during the tracing failure. \"\n            \"Ensure convert_frame.py calls tracer.output.cleanup_failed_trace() \"\n            \"in its except Exception block inside transform().\",\n        )\n\n        if scenario == \"cleanup_graph_on_failure\":\n            # After cleanup_failed_trace, graph nodes must have their doubly-linked\n            # list pointers (_prev, _next) set to None.  This breaks the cycle:\n            #   Graph._root <-> Node1 <-> Node2 <-> ... <-> Graph._root\n            # that would otherwise prevent immediate GC of the nodes.\n            prev_after = captured.get(\"node_prev_after\", [])\n            next_after = captured.get(\"node_next_after\", [])\n            for prev in prev_after:\n                self.assertIsNone(\n                    prev,\n                    \"Node._prev must be None after cleanup_failed_trace\",\n                )\n            for nxt in next_after:\n                self.assertIsNone(\n                    nxt,\n                    \"Node._next must be None after cleanup_failed_trace\",\n                )\n\n        elif scenario == \"cleanup_fake_tensor_refs\":\n            # After cleanup_failed_trace, tracked_fakes must be empty.\n            # This breaks the cycle:\n            #   FakeTensorMode -> ShapeEnv -> TrackedFake -> FakeTensor\n            #                                                -> FakeTensorMode\n            self.assertEqual(\n                captured.get(\"tracked_fakes_len_after\", -1),\n                0,\n                \"tracked_fakes must be empty after cleanup_failed_trace \"\n                \"to break the FakeTensorMode reference cycle\",\n            )\n\n        elif scenario == \"cleanup_shape_env_refs\":\n            # ShapeEnv.tracked_fakes is the SAME list object as\n            # OutputGraph.tracked_fakes.  Clearing it in cleanup_failed_trace\n            # therefore also clears the ShapeEnv's reference to TrackedFakes.\n            tracked_fakes = captured.get(\"tracked_fakes\")\n            self.assertIsNotNone(\n                tracked_fakes,\n                \"tracked_fakes must have been captured\",\n            )\n            # After cleanup, the list (shared with ShapeEnv) must be empty.\n            self.assertEqual(\n                len(tracked_fakes),\n                0,\n                \"ShapeEnv.tracked_fakes (same list as OutputGraph.tracked_fakes) \"\n                \"must be empty after cleanup_failed_trace\",\n            )\n\n        elif scenario == \"gc_not_needed_after_cleanup\":\n            # After cleanup_failed_trace breaks the reference cycles, objects\n            # should be collectable by reference counting alone — no full\n            # gc.collect() pass needed.\n            nodes = captured.get(\"nodes\", [])\n            node_refs = captured.get(\"node_refs\", [])\n            # Drop all remaining strong references to the nodes.\n            del nodes\n            captured.pop(\"nodes\", None)\n            captured.pop(\"node_refs\", None)\n            # With cycles broken, reference counting should collect them\n            # immediately.  We assert they are gone without calling gc.collect().\n            for ref in node_refs:\n                self.assertIsNone(\n                    ref(),\n                    \"Graph nodes must be immediately collectable (no gc.collect \"\n                    \"needed) after cleanup_failed_trace breaks their cycles\",\n                )\n\n        elif scenario == \"repeated_failure_no_leak\":\n            # Multiple tracing failures must not accumulate leaked objects.\n            for _ in range(3):\n                torch._dynamo.reset()\n                _run_tracing_failure()\n            # Force a full collection and verify nothing is in gc.garbage.\n            gc.collect()\n            self.assertEqual(\n                len(gc.garbage),\n                0,\n                \"No uncollectable garbage should remain after repeated \"\n                \"tracing failures\",\n            )\n\n\ninstantiate_parametrized_tests(TestGraphCleanup)\n\n\nif __name__ == \"__main__\":\n    from torch._dynamo.test_case import run_tests\n\n    run_tests()\n"},"caller":{"type":"direct"}}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":1,"cache_creation_input_tokens":1090,"cache_read_input_tokens":157129,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":1090,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":0},"output_tokens":8,"service_tier":"standard","inference_geo":"not_available"},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"f90ad9e6-7acf-4227-bdc4-db6ed200572f","uuid":"1e1d0534-801e-4b3c-9647-791f796471f0"}

These assignments will raise AttributeError before the fix is applied,\n    # which is intentional: the tests should fail before the fix.\n    original_cleanup = OutputGraph.cleanup_failed_trace\n\n    def patched_cleanup(self):\n        # Record weakrefs to key objects before cleanup\n        captured[\"og_ref\"] = weakref.ref(self)\n        captured[\"tracked_fakes\"] = self.tracked_fakes\n        captured[\"graph\"] = self.graph\n        # Record node refs and their _prev/_next before cleanup\n        nodes = list(self.graph.nodes)\n        captured[\"node_refs\"] = [weakref.ref(n) for n in nodes]\n        captured[\"nodes\"] = nodes\n        # Call the actual cleanup\n        original_cleanup(self)\n        # Record state after cleanup\n        captured[\"tracked_fakes_len_after\"] = len(self.tracked_fakes)\n        captured[\"node_prev_after\"] = [n._prev for n in nodes]\n        captured[\"node_next_after\"] = [n._next for n in nodes]\n\n    original_run = InstructionTranslator.run\n\n    def failing_run(self):\n        raise _ForcedTracingError(\"forced failure for test\")\n\n    OutputGraph.cleanup_failed_trace = patched_cleanup\n    InstructionTranslator.run = failing_run\n    try:\n        try:\n            def fn(x):\n                return x.sin()\n\n            torch.compile(fn, backend=\"eager\")(torch.randn(4))\n        except _ForcedTracingError:\n            pass\n    finally:\n        OutputGraph.cleanup_failed_trace = original_cleanup\n        InstructionTranslator.run = original_run\n\n    return captured\n\n\n@instantiate_parametrized_tests\nclass TestGraphCleanup(TestCase):\n    @parametrize(\n        \"scenario\",\n        [\n            \"cleanup_graph_on_failure\",\n            \"cleanup_fake_tensor_refs\",\n            \"cleanup_shape_env_refs\",\n            \"gc_not_needed_after_cleanup\",\n            \"repeated_failure_no_leak\",\n        ],\n    )\n    def test_parametrization(self, scenario):\n        \"\"\"\n        Test that cleanup_failed_trace() correctly breaks reference cycles\n        that would otherwise prevent garbage collection after a failed trace.\n        \"\"\"\n        torch._dynamo.reset()\n\n        # This will raise AttributeError if cleanup_failed_trace does not exist\n        # (i.e., before the fix is applied), causing the test to fail.\n        captured = _run_tracing_failure()\n\n        if scenario == \"cleanup_graph_on_failure\":\n            # After cleanup, all graph nodes should have their doubly-linked\n            # list pointers (_prev, _next) set to None, breaking the cycle:\n            #   Graph._root -> Node -> ... -> Graph._root\n            prev_after = captured.get(\"node_prev_after\", [])\n            next_after = captured.get(\"node_next_after\", [])\n            for prev in prev_after:\n                self.assertIsNone(prev)\n            for nxt in next_after:\n                self.assertIsNone(nxt)\n\n        elif scenario == \"cleanup_fake_tensor_refs\":\n            # After cleanup, tracked_fakes must be empty, breaking the cycle:\n            #   FakeTensorMode -> ShapeEnv -> TrackedFake -> FakeTensor -> FakeTensorMode\n            self.assertEqual(\n                captured.get(\"tracked_fakes_len_after\", -1),\n                0,\n                \"tracked_fakes should be empty after cleanup_failed_trace\",\n            )\n\n        elif scenario == \"cleanup_shape_env_refs\":\n            # ShapeEnv.tracked_fakes is the same list object as\n            # OutputGraph.tracked_fakes, so clearing it clears the ShapeEnv\n            # references too.\n            tracked_fakes = captured.get(\"tracked_fakes\")\n            if tracked_fakes is not None:\n                # The list should be empty after cleanup (it was cleared in-place)\n                self.assertEqual(\n                    len(tracked_fakes),\n                    0,\n                    \"ShapeEnv tracked_fakes should be empty after cleanup\",\n                )\n\n        elif scenario == \"gc_not_needed_after_cleanup\":\n            # After cleanup_failed_trace breaks reference cycles, objects\n            # should be collectable by reference counting alone (no gc.collect()\n            # needed). We verify this by checking that nodes with no other\n            # strong references are dead even without gc.collect().\n            nodes = captured.get(\"nodes\", [])\n            node_refs = captured.get(\"node_refs\", [])\n            # Drop strong references to the nodes\n            del nodes\n            captured.pop(\"nodes\", None)\n            captured.pop(\"node_refs\", None)\n            # Without gc.collect(), reference-counted cleanup should suffice\n            # because the cycles were broken by cleanup_failed_trace.\n            for ref in node_refs:\n                # If cleanup_failed_trace broke the cycle, ref counting\n                # means ref() should already be None.\n                # (We allow gc.collect here only as a fallback check)\n                self.assertIsNone(\n                    ref(),\n                    \"Graph nodes should be collectable without a full GC pass\",\n                )\n\n        elif scenario == \"repeated_failure_no_leak\":\n            # Multiple tracing failures should not accumulate leaked objects.\n            for _ in range(3):\n                torch._dynamo.reset()\n                _run_tracing_failure()\n            # Verify no uncollectable garbage accumulated\n            gc.collect()\n            self.assertEqual(\n                len(gc.garbage),\n                0,\n                \"No uncollectable garbage should remain after repeated failures\",\n            )\n\n\ninstantiate_parametrized_tests(TestGraphCleanup)\n\n\nif __name__ == \"__main__\":\n    from torch._dynamo.test_case import run_tests\n\n    run_tests()\n"}}

Additionally, FakeTensorMode ->\n           ShapeEnv -> TrackedFake -> FakeTensor -> FakeTensorMode creates a cycle.\n\n           Manually cleaning up these cycles allows GC without a full gc pass.\n It holds a root SubgraphTracer where the FX graph is built.\\n3245: #\\n3246: # HigherOrderOperators are operators that take functions as their arguments.\\n3247: # When Dynamo encounters a HigherOrderOperator, then it attempts to introspect\\n3248: # the function passed to it (call this the \\\"body function\\\"), capture it into a\\n3249: # GraphModule, and rewrite the call to the HigherOrderOperator to use the\\n3250: # GraphModule.\\n3251: #\\n3252: # The way we handle the capture of body functions is through having\\n3253: # (possibly nested) SubgraphTracers, one per body function.\\n3254: #\\n3255: # Mechanically, we do the introspection by:\\n3256: # - Creating a new SubgraphTracer via OutputGraph.subtracer\\n3257: # - Executing the body function.\\n3258: # This constructs the graph of the body function in the new SubgraphTracer\\n3259: # while modifying the state of the OutputGraph. Mainly the\\n378:     generated fx.Graph.\\n379: \\n380:     OutputGraph is 1:1 with a frame being processed. Each frame is associated\\n381:     with some root InstructionTranslator. When user code calls a function,\\n382:     we construct a InliningInstructionTranslator that continues to write into\\n383:     the root InstructionTranslator's OutputGraph.\\n384:     \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n385: \\n386:     side_effects: SideEffects\\n387: \\n388:     def __init__(\\n389:         self,\\n390:         code_options: dict[str, Any],\\n391:         compiler_fn: Optional[CompilerFn],\\n392:         root_tx: \\\"InstructionTranslatorBase\\\",\\n393:         export: bool,\\n394:         export_constraints: Sequence[_ConstraintTarget],\\n395:         frame_state: Any,\\n396:         local_scope: Scope,\\n397:         global_scope: Scope,\\n398:         f_code: CodeType,\\n399:         torch_function_mode_stack: list[torch.overrides.TorchFunctionMode],\\n400:         package: Optional[\\\"CompilePackage\\\"],\\n401:     ) -> None:\\n402:         super().__init__(\\n403:             local_scope,\\n404:             global_scope,\\n405:             torch_function_mode_stack,\\n406:             guard_on_key_order=set(),\\n407:             input_source_to_sizes_strides={},\\n408:             dual_level=torch.autograd.forward_ad._current_level,\\n409:             functorch_layers=torch._functorch.pyfunctorch.retrieve_all_functorch_interpreters(),\\n410:             current_device=torch.utils._device.CURRENT_DEVICE,\\n411:             # Mainly the\n378:     generated fx.Graph.\n379: \n380:     OutputGraph is 1:1 with a frame being processed. Each frame is associated\n381:     with some root InstructionTranslator. StackLocalsMetadata]:\n1183:         \"\"\"\n1184:         Gets the stack + locals values belonging to tx that need to be restored.\n1185: \n1186:         Also prunes dead tx locals and realizes all stack\n1208:             if len(tx.stack) - i <= stack_pops:\n1209:                 stack_values.append(value)\n1210:                 continue\n1211:             if isinstance(value, NullVariable):\n1212:                 meta.stack_null_idxes.append(i)\n1213:             else:\n1214:                 stack_values.append(value)\n1215:             if isinstance(value, prune_dead_locals\n1228:         # will clear out all of symbolic_locals because RETURN_VALUE is the\n1229:         # last instruction and no more locals are used.  The fanciness here\n1230:         # is only needed for partial graphs.\n1231:         # NOTE: All cell and free variables are represented as CellVariable,\n1232:         # so checks for NULLs and context managers in the case of codegen'ing resume\n1233:         # functions will not be performed on them. This is expected behavior.\n1234:         for k, v in tx.symbolic_locals.items():\n1235:             # Note! this explicitly uses .local_name for matching\n1236:             # Failure to do so will cause spurious registrations in val_to_names.\n1237:             # This will in turn result in spurious variables showing up in the graph.\n1238:             # This was very tricky to debug. Call the compiled subgraph\n1277:             - Apply side effects\n1278:             - Codegen stack and locals\n1279:             - Store the locals\n1280: \n1281:         Python does not allow NULL to be an arg to a function, so we do not codegen NULLs on the stack,\n1282:         unless the value is one of the top `stack_pops` values on the stack (these values are expected to be\n1283:         popped immediately after this generated code. The prologue of the resume function is expected to restore\n1284:         any dropped NULLs.\n1285: \n1286:         Returns stack indices and locals keys where we dropped NULLs, and where we found inactive context manager objects.\n1287:         \"\"\"\n1288: \n1289:         assert self.root_tx is not None\n1290: \n1291:         # FIXME temporary assert to make sure we're not accidentally compiling nested graph breaks\n1292:         # before we're done the full implementation\n1293:         assert self.root_tx is tx\n1294: \n1295:         # bytecode tracing has finished. Exit from all context manager variables to make sure global state is restored\n1333:         for block in reversed(self.root_tx.block_stack):\n1334:             block.exit(self.root_tx, is_graph_break=reason.graph_break)\n1335: \n1336:         self.cleanup_graph()\n1337: \n1338:         # stack values and restore vars for each frame are pushed in reverse order\n1339:         # i.e. last element corresponds to root frame, first element corresponds to current frame\n1340:         all_stack_values = []\n1341:         all_restore_vars = []\n1342:         all_stack_locals_metas = []\n1343:         cur_tx: Optional[InstructionTranslatorBase] = tx\n1344:         while True:\n1345:             assert cur_tx is not None\n1346:             # this should have been checked by the caller\n1347:             assert all(block.can_restore() for block in cur_tx.block_stack)\n1348:             stack_values, restore_vars, meta = self._get_stack_values_to_restore(\n1349:                 cur_tx, stack_pops\n1350:             )\n1351:             all_stack_values.append(stack_values)\n1352:             all_restore_vars.append(restore_vars)\n1353:             all_stack_locals_metas.append(meta)\n1354:             if cur_tx is self.root_tx:\n1355:                 break\n1356:             cur_tx = tx.parent\n1357: \n1358:         # Use nn.Module \"proxies\" in the constructed GraphModule so that\n1359:         # the resulting GM does not hold additional strong references to the original modules.\n1360:         # This prevents a strong ref cycle where Dynamo created code holds on to references\n1361:         # Basically we have this problem\\n2150:         # where our compiler tends to choke when we have unused inputs. The way\\n2151:         # we support dynamic float arguments is by doing a joint fx pass and\\n2152:         # tensorifying away as many symfloats as we can. The way\n2151:         # we support dynamic float arguments is by doing a joint fx pass and\n2152:         # tensorifying away as many symfloats as we can. to source all the inputs.\\n2753:         #\\n2754:         # In export, this is bad, because you want a self-contained export\\n2755:         # object which only depends on the inputs you explicitly passed to it.\\n2756:         # So we are a bit more strict about what sources can become inputs\\n2757:         # in export\\n2758:         if self.is_export and self.parent is None:\\n2759:             assert source is not None\\n2760:             if not is_from_local_source(source, only_allow_input=True):\\n2761:                 self.output_graph.source_to_user_stacks.setdefault(source, []).append(\\n2762:                     TracingContext.extract_stack()\\n2763:                 )\\n2764: \\n2765:         # So, record the `target`\\n2790:             # name as well in the _used_names to prevent any collision.\\n2791:             self._used_names.add(name)\\n2792: \\n2793:             # NOTE: [Auto lift basic free symbols when create_graph_input]\\n2794:             # Whenever we call create_graph_input, we try to also lift the basic symbols in example values\\n2795:             # as graph input.\\n2796:             # This applies to both top-level graph and subgraphs in higher order ops.\\n2797:             # It has several cases:\\n2798:             #  1. When create_graph_input for a tensor that has symbolic shapes,\\n2799:             #     we look for basic symbols in its size and stride, we check if the symbol is bound\\n2800:             #     in current graph (i.e. bound_symbols), it it's not bound, we'll create a placeholder\\n2801:             #     for it then recursively check its parent, creates ph if not bound.\\n2802:             #     Every tracer maintains a mapping (i.e. lifted_freevars)\\n2803:             #     that maps from parent proxy to proxy in current tracer for the symbol.\\n2804:             #  2. When create_graph_input for a tensor with unbacked symbolic shapes,\\n2805:             #     Backed symbols all come from inputs's symbolic shape. But unbacked symbols\\n2806:             #     can be created while tracing. So we use track_unbacked_symbols will intercept\\n2807:             #     at wrap_fx_proxy, and try to bind the unbacked symbols immediately after they're\\n2808:             #     created.\\n2809:             #  3. subgraph will also lifted basic symbols in compound exprs of tensor shape.\\n2810:             #     For example, if an input to subgraph takes size [s1+s2//8], we'll look for the\\n2811:             #     the free symbols in the sizes and lift as inputs similar to 1 in _lift_symbols_in_symint)\\n2812:             #  4. When the symint is a\\n2814:             #     compound expr, we'll just create the proxy for the compouned expr but not lift its basic symbols.\\n2815:             # Also see NOTE: [Export inputs must be explicitly passed in]\\n2816:             is_strict_export = self.is_export\\n2817:             is_non_strict_export = torch.compiler.is_compiling()\\n2818:             if not is_strict_export and not is_non_strict_export:\\n2819:                 if isinstance(example_value, torch.Tensor):\\n2820:                     self._lift_basic_symbols(example_value, source)\\n2821:                 elif isinstance(example_value, (list, tuple)):\\n2822:                     for i, e in enumerate(example_value):\\n2823:                         if not isinstance(e, torch.Tensor):\\n2824:                             continue\\n2825: \\n2826:                         e_source = None\\n2827:                         if source:\\n2828:                             e_source = GetItemSource(\\n2829:                                 base=source, index=i, index_is_slice=False\\n2830:                             )\\n2831: \\n2832:                         self._lift_basic_symbols(e, e_source)\\n2833: \\n2834:             # See NOTE: [Nested SubgraphTracer and free_variable handling] for more details\\n2842:     def lift_tracked_freevar_to_input(\\n2843:         self, proxy: fx.Proxy\\n2844:     ) -> Union[LazyProxy, fx.Proxy]:\\n2845:         # we automatically lift basic symbols in the sizes/strides of a tensor t.\\n2856:         # Suppose parent graph calls sz = t.size()[0], it creates\\n2857:         # a proxy in parent and the subgraph accesses sz via closure. sz's proxy is not tracked\\n2858:         # So we are a bit more strict about what sources can become inputs\\n2757:         # in export\\n2758:         if self.is_export and self.parent is None:\\n2759:             assert source is not None\\n2760:             if not is_from_local_source(source, only_allow_input=True):\\n2761:                 self.output_graph.source_to_user_stacks.setdefault(source, []).append(\\n2762:                     TracingContext.extract_stack()\\n2763:                 )\\n2764: \\n2765:         # This ensures that we do not have a name\\n2767:         # collision.\\n2768:         name = get_unique_name_wrt(name, self._used_names)\\n2769:         if self.input_name_to_proxy:\\n2770:             prev_name = next(reversed(self.input_name_to_proxy))\\n2771:             node = self.input_name_to_proxy[prev_name].node\\n2772:             if before:\\n2773:                 ctx = self.graph.inserting_before(node)\\n2774:             else:\\n2775:                 ctx = self.graph.inserting_after(node)\\n2776:         else:\\n2777:             ctx = self.graph.inserting_before(None)\\n2778:         with ctx:\\n2779:             proxy = self.create_proxy(\\\"placeholder\\\", name, (), {}, type_expr=type_expr)\\n2780:             set_example_value(proxy.node, example_value)\\n2781:             if self.input_name_to_proxy and before:\\n2782:                 k, v = self.input_name_to_proxy.popitem()\\n2783:                 self.input_name_to_proxy[name] = proxy\\n2784:                 self.input_name_to_proxy[k] = v\\n2785:             else:\\n2786:                 self.input_name_to_proxy[name] = proxy\\n2787: \\n2788:             # For placeholder nodes, `name` is passed as a str to the target,\\n2789:             # and then torch.fx decides the node.name. So, record the `target`\\n2790:             # name as well in the _used_names to prevent any collision.\\n2791:             self._used_names.add(name)\\n2792: \\n2793:             # NOTE: [Auto lift basic free symbols when create_graph_input]\\n2794:             # Whenever we call create_graph_input, we try to also lift the basic symbols in example values\\n2795:             # as graph input.\\n2796:             # This applies to both top-level graph and subgraphs in higher order ops.\\n2797:             # It has several cases:\\n2798:             #  1. When create_graph_input for a tensor that has symbolic shapes,\\n2799:             #     we look for basic symbols in its size and stride, we check if the symbol is bound\\n2800:             #     in current graph (i.e. bound_symbols), it it's not bound, we'll create a placeholder\\n2801:             #     for it then recursively check its parent, creates ph if not bound.\\n2802:             #     Every tracer maintains a mapping (i.e. lifted_freevars)\\n2803:             #     that maps from parent proxy to proxy in current tracer for the symbol.\\n2804:             #  2. When create_graph_input for a tensor with unbacked symbolic shapes,\\n2805:             #     Backed symbols all come from inputs's symbolic shape. But unbacked symbols\\n2806:             #     can be created while tracing. So we use track_unbacked_symbols will intercept\\n2807:             #     at wrap_fx_proxy, and try to bind the unbacked symbols immediately after they're\\n2808:             #     created.\\n2809:             #  3. subgraph will also lifted basic symbols in compound exprs of tensor shape.\\n2810:             #     For example, if an input to subgraph takes size [s1+s2//8], we'll look for the\\n2811:             #     the free symbols in the sizes and lift as inputs similar to 1 in _lift_symbols_in_symint)\\n2812:             #  4. When create_graph_input for a SymInt, if the symint is a basic symbol, we'll track it\\n2813:             #     in bound_symbols so that we don't lift the same basic symbol twice. When the symint is a\\n2814:             #     compound expr, we'll just create the proxy for the compouned expr but not lift its basic symbols.\\n2815:             # Also see NOTE: [Export inputs must be explicitly passed in]\\n2816:             is_strict_export = self.is_export\\n2817:             is_non_strict_export = torch.compiler.is_compiling()\\n2818:             if not is_strict_export and not is_non_strict_export:\\n2819:                 if isinstance(example_value, torch.Tensor):\\n2820:                     self._lift_basic_symbols(example_value, source)\\n2821:                 elif isinstance(example_value, (list, tuple)):\\n2822:                     for i, e in enumerate(example_value):\\n2823:                         if not isinstance(e, torch.Tensor):\\n2824:                             continue\\n2825: \\n2826:                         e_source = None\\n2827:                         if source:\\n2828:                             e_source = GetItemSource(\\n2829:                                 base=source, index=i, index_is_slice=False\\n2830:                             )\\n2831: \\n2832:                         self._lift_basic_symbols(e, e_source)\\n2833: \\n2834:             # we automatically lift basic symbols in the sizes/strides of a tensor t.\\n2856:         # Suppose parent graph calls sz = t.size()[0], it creates\\n2857:         # a proxy in parent and the subgraph accesses sz via closure. sz's proxy is not tracked\\n2858:         # in current sub-tracer so we may lift the same symbol twice.\\n2859:         if (\\n2860:             isinstance(example_value, torch.SymInt)\\n2861:             and example_value.node.expr in self.bound_symbols\\n2862:         ):\\n2863:             return self.bound_symbols[example_value.node.expr]\\n2864: \\n2865:         # So we use track_unbacked_symbols will intercept\n2807:             #     at wrap_fx_proxy, and try to bind the unbacked symbols immediately after they're\n2808:             #     created.\n2809:             #  3. subgraph will also lifted basic symbols in compound exprs of tensor shape.\n2810:             #     For example, if an input to subgraph takes size [s1+s2//8], we'll look for the\n2811:             #     the free symbols in the sizes and lift as inputs similar to 1 in _lift_symbols_in_symint)\n2812:             #  4. # NOTE: [HigherOrderOperator tracing design]\\n3242: # Ignoring HigherOrderOperators for a moment,\\n3243: # OutputGraph represents the graph being built by Dynamo that may be compiled\\n3244: # and executed. It holds a root SubgraphTracer where the FX graph is built.\\n3245: #\\n3246: # HigherOrderOperators are operators that take functions as their arguments.\\n3247: # When Dynamo encounters a HigherOrderOperator, then it attempts to introspect\\n3248: # the function passed to it (call this the \\\"body function\\\"), capture it into a\\n3249: # GraphModule, and rewrite the call to the HigherOrderOperator to use the\\n3250: # GraphModule.\\n3251: #\\n3252: # The way we handle the capture of body functions is through having\\n3253: # (possibly nested) SubgraphTracers, one per body function.\\n3254: #\\n3255: # Mechanically, we do the introspection by:\\n3256: # - Creating a new SubgraphTracer via OutputGraph.subtracer\\n3257: # - Executing the body function.\\n3258: # This constructs the graph of the body function in the new SubgraphTracer\\n3259: # while modifying the state of the OutputGraph.   OutputGraph.side_effects\\n3264: # - guards produced by the body function get accumulated into OutputGraph.guards\\n3265: #\\n3266: # The traced function has some special properties that make it easier for us\\n3267: # # NOTE: [HigherOrderOperator tracing design]\\n3242: # Ignoring HigherOrderOperators for a moment,\\n3243: # OutputGraph represents the graph being built by Dynamo that may be compiled\\n3244: # and executed. It holds a root SubgraphTracer where the FX graph is built.\\n3245: #\\n3246: # HigherOrderOperators are operators that take functions as their arguments.\\n3247: # When Dynamo encounters a HigherOrderOperator, then it attempts to introspect\\n3248: # the function passed to it (call this the \\\"body function\\\"), capture it into a\\n3249: # GraphModule, and rewrite the call to the HigherOrderOperator to use the\\n3250: # GraphModule.\\n3251: #\\n3252: # The way we handle the capture of body functions is through having\\n3253: # (possibly nested) SubgraphTracers, one per body function.\\n3254: #\\n3255: # Mechanically, we do the introspection by:\\n3256: # - Creating a new SubgraphTracer via OutputGraph.subtracer\\n3257: # - Executing the body function.\\n3258: # This constructs the graph of the body function in the new SubgraphTracer\\n3259: # while modifying the state of the OutputGraph. For example:\\n3260: # - the OutputGraph can receive new GraphArgs (if we discover any new\\n3261: #   untracked Tensors)\\n3262: # - side effects from the body function get accumulated into\\n3263: # It holds a root SubgraphTracer where the FX graph is built.\n3245: #\n3246: # HigherOrderOperators are operators that take functions as their arguments.\n3247: # When Dynamo encounters a HigherOrderOperator, then it attempts to introspect\n3248: # the function passed to it (call this the \"body function\"), capture it into a\n3249: # GraphModule, and rewrite the call to the HigherOrderOperator to use the\n3250: # GraphModule.\n3251: #\n3252: # The way we handle the capture of body functions is through having\n3253: # (possibly nested) SubgraphTracers, one per body function.\n3254: #\n3255: # Mechanically, we do the introspection by:\n3256: # - Creating a new SubgraphTracer via OutputGraph.subtracer\n3257: # - Executing the body function.\n3258: # This constructs the graph of the body function in the new SubgraphTracer\n3259: # while modifying the state of the OutputGraph. f_globals[\"__builtins__\"] can be a dict or a module. This is an\n   357→    # implementation detail -\n   358→    # https://docs.python.org/3/library/builtins.html.\n   359→\n   360→    # This makes guarding on any builtin messy because the guard check_fn\n   361→    # has to check if the __builtins__ is a module or dict, and then access\n   362→    # by either using getattr or getitem respectively.\n   363→\n   364→    # To solve this problem, we insert a new entry in f_globals which points\n   365→    # to the builtins __dict__ and then we guard any builtin on this dict.\n   366→    # To avoid any collision with the pre-existing keys, we use the\n   367→    # install_global to give us a unique dict key.\n   368→\n   369→    f_builtins = global_scope[\"__builtins__\"]\n   370→    if not isinstance(f_builtins, dict):\n   371→        f_builtins = f_builtins.__dict__\n   372→    return f_builtins\n   373→\n   374→\n\n<system-reminder>\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. If list inputs are stolen, but still needed after the function call, create aliases to keep them alive\n        maybe_gm = self.local_scope.get(\"self\")\n        stolen_list_names = get_locals_to_steal(maybe_gm)\n        if not stolen_list_names:\n            return [], {}\n\n        alias_insts = []\n        needs_alias: dict[str, list[VariableTracker]] = {}\n\n        queue = [\n            *tx.stack,\n            *tx.symbolic_locals.values(),\n            *self.side_effects.store_attr_mutations.keys(),\n        ]\n\n        while queue:\n            x = queue.pop()\n            if isinstance(x, BaseListVariable):\n                assert isinstance(x.items, list)\n                queue += x.items\n                continue\n\n            if not (\n                (\n                    x not in self.side_effects.store_attr_mutations\n                    or isinstance(x.mutation_type, AttributeMutationExisting)\n                )\n                and isinstance(x.source, GetItemSource)\n                and isinstance(x.source.base, LocalSource)\n                and x.source.base.local_name in stolen_list_names\n            ):\n                continue\n\n            stolen_name = x.source.base.local_name\n            if stolen_name not in needs_alias:\n                needs_alias[stolen_name] = []\n            needs_alias[stolen_name].append(x)\n\n        visited = {}\n        overridden_sources: dict[Source, Source] = {}\n        for arg in self.graphargs:\n            if not (\n                isinstance(arg._example, list)\n                and isinstance(arg.source, LocalSource)\n                and arg.source.local_name in needs_alias\n            ):\n                continue\n\n            # arg is a list that will be cleared by the compiled function\n            list_name = arg.source.local_name\n            assert list_name in self.code_options[\"co_varnames\"]\n            for x in needs_alias[list_name]:\n                # Skip if already handled.\n                if x.source in overridden_sources:\n                    continue\n\n                # A small codegen optimization because we might have different\n                # VariableTrackers that share the same source.\n                list_idx = x.source.index  # type: ignore[attr-defined]\n                if list_idx not in visited:\n                    alias_name = self.new_var(\n                        f\"{list_name}_ref\"\n                    )  # self.new_var already adds unique id suffix\n\n                    visited[list_idx] = alias_name\n                    # bytecode of `alias_name = list_name[list_idx]`\n                    alias_insts.extend(\n                        [\n                            create_instruction(\"LOAD_FAST\", argval=list_name),\n                            create_load_const(list_idx),\n                            create_instruction(\"BINARY_SUBSCR\"),\n                            create_instruction(\"STORE_FAST\", argval=alias_name),\n                        ]\n                    )\n\n                # operate on alias, handled by suffix codegen\n                old_source = x.source\n                overridden_sources[old_source] = LocalSource(visited[list_idx])\n\n        # NOTE: we need `overridden_sources` because (1) we want to codegen for\n        # these list items to use the new local source, but (2) we want to avoid\n        # updating `source` in place because that might break invariants in\n        # other parts of Dynamo like guards.\n        return alias_insts, overridden_sources\n\n    def _get_stack_values_to_restore(\n        self, tx: \"InstructionTranslatorBase\", stack_pops: int\n    ) -> tuple[list[VariableTracker], list[str], StackLocalsMetadata]:\n        \"\"\"\n        Gets the stack + locals values belonging to tx that need to be restored.\n\n        Also prunes dead tx locals and realizes all VTs in the tx's stack.\n\n        NullVariables in stack/locals will NOT be restored, unless they are the top `stack_pops`\n        elements of the stack - it is expected that the next instruction to run will pop the top\n        `stack_pops` elements of the stack, so we should codegen NULLs.\n\n        Returns:\n            - stack_values: stack and locals values that need to be restored\n            - restore_vars: names of locals corresponding to the locals part of `stack_values`\n            - meta: locations of NULLs and ContextWrappingVariables in the stack/locals\n                (ignores the top `stack_pops` values on the stack)\n        \"\"\"\n        tx.prune_dead_locals()\n\n        stack_values = []\n        meta = StackLocalsMetadata()\n\n        # realize any unrealized tensor VTs in case they\n        # need to be added to self.nn_modules as attributes\n        for i, value in enumerate(tx.stack):\n            variables.LazyVariableTracker.realize_all(value)\n            # ignore top `stack_pops` values on the stack\n            if len(tx.stack) - i <= stack_pops:\n                stack_values.append(value)\n                continue\n            if isinstance(value, NullVariable):\n                meta.stack_null_idxes.append(i)\n            else:\n                stack_values.append(value)\n            if isinstance(value, ContextWrappingVariable):\n                target_values = (\n                    () if value.target_values is None else tuple(value.target_values)\n                )\n                # NOTE: track index in stack after NULLs have been removed\n                meta.stack_ctx_args.append((len(stack_values) - 1, target_values))\n                meta.stack_ctx_idxes_orig.append(i)\n\n        # Add all the local vars to the \"stack\" so restore at the end\n        restore_vars: list[str] = []\n        val_to_names: dict[VariableTracker, list[str]] = {}\n        # NB: Typically (i.e., for graph compile from RETURN_VALUE),\n        # symbolic_locals will be empty at this point, as prune_dead_locals\n        # will clear out all of symbolic_locals because RETURN_VALUE is the\n        # last instruction and no more locals are used.  The fanciness here\n        # is only needed for partial graphs.\n        # NOTE: All cell and free variables are represented as CellVariable,\n        # so checks for NULLs and context managers in the case of codegen'ing resume\n        # functions will not be performed on them. This is expected behavior.\n        for k, v in tx.symbolic_locals.items():\n            # Note! this explicitly uses .local_name for matching\n            # Failure to do so will cause spurious registrations in val_to_names.\n            # This will in turn result in spurious variables showing up in the graph.\n            # This was very tricky to debug. For an example, dump the graph at call_user_compiler\n            # while running test_subgraphs.py\n            if isinstance(v.source, LocalSource) and v.source.local_name == k:\n                continue  # no need to restore initial state\n            if isinstance(v, CellVariable) and v.local_name == k:\n                continue  # no need to restore initial state\n            # Do not load variable if it is NULL.\n            if sys.version_info >= (3, 12):\n                # type: ignore[attr-defined]\n                )\n\n                _LazyGraphModule.force_recompile(lazy_gm)\n\n                if not isinstance(compiled_fn, _LazyGraphModule):\n                    # replace compiled_fn with the real forward method\n                    compiled_fn = lazy_gm.forward\n\n            if self.package is not None:\n                self.package.add_backend_id(name, compiled_fn)\n\n            compiled_fn = disable(\n                compiled_fn, reason=\"do not trace Dynamo-compiled graph\"\n            )\n\n            counters[\"stats\"][\"unique_graphs\"] += 1\n            assert old_fake_mode.shape_env is not None\n            if specializations := old_fake_mode.shape_env.specializations:\n                specialization_guards = []\n                specialization_cache: dict[Specialization, Callable[[Any], Any]] = {}\n                sources = [a.source for a in self.graphargs]\n                for specialization in specializations:\n                    source_index = sources.index(specialization.source)\n                    check_fn_source = inspect.getsource(specialization.check_fn).strip()\n                    # Required because the LABDA_GUARD API requires a root guard manager\n                    unused_root_guard_manager = RootGuardManager()\n                    check_fn = guards.LAMBDA_GUARD(  # type: ignore[attr-defined]\n                        unused_root_guard_manager,\n                        specialization.check_fn,\n                        [check_fn_source],\n                    )\n\n                    log.debug(\n                        \"Compiling backend specialized graph with specialization=%s\",\n                        check_fn_source,\n                    )\n\n                    specialization_guards.append(\n                        (\n                            functools.partial(\n                                lambda idx, args, check_fn=check_fn: check_fn(\n                                    args[idx]\n                                ),\n                                source_index,\n                            ),\n                            specialization,\n                        )\n                    )\n\n                @torch._dynamo.disable(reason=\"do not trace Dynamo-compiled graph\")  # type: ignore[misc]\n                def specialized_dispatch(*args: Any, **kwargs: Any) -> Any:\n                    for check_fn, specialization in specialization_guards:\n                        if check_fn(args):\n                            if specialization in specialization_cache:\n                                return specialization_cache[specialization](\n                                    *args, **kwargs\n                                )\n\n                            with self.shape_env.patch_source_specialization(\n                                specialization.source, specialization.check_fn\n                            ):\n                                # Modify gm so AOTAutogradCache key changes per specialization\n                                gm.meta[\"specialization\"] = specialization\n                                example_inputs: list[Tensor] = list(args)\n                                with tracing(self.tracing_context):\n                                    specialization_cache[specialization] = (\n                                        self.call_user_compiler(gm, example_inputs)\n                                    )\n\n                            return specialization_cache[specialization](*args, **kwargs)\n                    return compiled_fn(*args, **kwargs)\n\n                # This is safe because we pre-process name to be unique\n                self.install_global_unsafe(name, specialized_dispatch)\n            else:\n                # This is safe because we pre-process name to be unique\n                self.install_global_unsafe(name, compiled_fn)\n\n            assert self.root_tx is not None\n            cg = PyCodegen(self.root_tx)\n            cg.make_call_generated_code(name)\n            return cg.get_instructions()\n\n    @property\n    def placeholders(self) -> list[fx.Node]:\n        return self.graph.find_nodes(op=\"placeholder\")\n\n    @property\n    def graphargs(self) -> list[GraphArg]:\n        return [node.meta[\"grapharg\"] for node in self.placeholders]\n\n    def call_user_compiler(\n        self, gm: fx.GraphModule, example_inputs: list[Tensor]\n    ) -> CompiledFn:\n        with dynamo_timed(\n            \"OutputGraph.call_user_compiler\",\n            phase_name=\"backend_compile\",\n            log_pt2_compile_event=True,\n            log_waitcounter=True,\n            waitcounter_name_override=\"compile_aot_autograd\",\n            dynamo_compile_column_us=\"aot_autograd_cumulative_compile_time_us\",\n        ):\n            return self._call_user_compiler(gm, example_inputs)\n\n    def _call_user_compiler(\n        self, gm: fx.GraphModule, example_inputs: list[Tensor]\n    ) -> CompiledFn:\n        assert self.compiler_fn is not None\n        tot = 0\n        placeholders = []\n        for node in gm.graph.nodes:\n            if node.op in (\"call_function\", \"call_method\", \"call_module\"):\n                tot += 1\n            if node.op == \"placeholder\":\n                placeholders.append(node)\n        increment_op_count(tot)\n        for pl in placeholders:\n            if not hasattr(pl, \"_dynamo_source\"):\n                arg = pl.meta[\"grapharg\"]\n                # TODO: Why isn't this stored in meta :think:\n                # NOTE: can't move these into meta: https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/issues/141640\n                pl._dynamo_source = arg.source\n\n        # NOTE: can't move these into meta: https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/issues/141640\n        gm._param_name_to_source = self.param_name_to_source  # type: ignore[assignment]\n        gm._source_to_user_stacks = self.source_to_user_stacks  # type: ignore[assignment]\n\n        name = (\n            self.compiler_fn.__name__\n            if hasattr(self.compiler_fn, \"__name__\")\n            else \"<unknown compiler_fn>\"\n        )\n        try:\n            _step_logger()(logging.INFO, f\"calling compiler function {name}\")\n            compiler_fn = self.compiler_fn\n            if config.verify_correctness:\n                compiler_fn = WrapperBackend(compiler_fn)\n            compiled_fn = compiler_fn(gm, example_inputs)\n            _step_logger()(logging.INFO, f\"done compiler function {name}\")\n            assert callable(compiled_fn), \"compiler_fn did not return callable\"\n        except (TensorifyScalarRestartAnalysis, ShortenTraceback):\n            raise\n        except exceptions_allowed_to_be_fallback as e:\n            if self.has_user_defined_allowed_in_graph:\n                raise BackendCompilerFailed(\n                    self.compiler_fn, e, inspect.currentframe()\n                ).with_traceback(e.__traceback__) from None\n            unimplemented_v2_with_warning(\n                e,\n                self.root_tx.f_code,\n                gb_type=\"Backend compiler exception\",\n                context=f\"Backend: {name}\\nException:{str(e)}\\nTraceback:\\n{self.root_tx.format_frame_summary()}\",\n                explanation=f\"Backend compiler `{name}` failed with {str(e)}. Adding a graph break.\",\n                hints=[\n                    \"Report an issue to the backend compiler repo.\",\n                ],\n            )\n        except SkipFrame as e:\n            # The backend compiler has requested that we skip the frame, instead of\n            # aborting execution.\n            raise e\n        except Exception as e:\n            raise BackendCompilerFailed(\n                self.compiler_fn, e, inspect.currentframe()\n            ).with_traceback(e.__traceback__) from None\n\n        signpost_event(\n            \"dynamo\",\n            \"OutputGraph.call_user_compiler\",\n            {\n                **self.co_fields,\n                \"op_count\": tot,\n                \"node_count\": len(gm.graph.nodes),\n                \"input_count\": len(placeholders),\n            },\n        )\n\n        return compiled_fn\n\n    def dedup_pass(self) -> dict[str, torch.fx.GraphModule]:\n        if torch._dynamo.config.use_graph_deduplication:\n            return apply_graph_deduplication(self)\n        else:\n            return {}\n\n    def install_subgraph(self, name: str, sub_gm: torch.fx.GraphModule) -> str:\n        next_name = get_unique_name_wrt(name, self.nn_modules, requires_suffix=True)\n        sub_gm.__name__ = next_name  # type: ignore[assignment]\n        sub_gm.torchdynamo_force_dynamic = False  # type: ignore[assignment]\n        # This graph module is not present in the user space, so it can't be\n        # accessed by a source. Set source=None.\n        self.register_attr_or_module(sub_gm, next_name, source=None)\n        return next_name\n\n    def example_inputs(self) -> list[torch.Tensor]:\n        result = [arg.example for arg in self.graphargs]\n        return result\n\n    def remove_unused_get_attr_nodes(self) -> None:\n        for node in sorted(self.graph.find_nodes(op=\"get_attr\"), reverse=True):\n            if len(list(node.users)) == 0:\n                self.remove_node(node)\n\n    def remove_unused_graphargs(self) -> None:\n        # NB: It's OK to drop GraphArg for symbols that ended up being\n        # specialized iff they are not used in runtime assertions.  You don't\n        # even have to make a guard for it, because ShapeEnv produce_guards\n        # operates on tracked_fakes, which never gets pruned.\n        # That being said, you'll get marginally better generated\n        # guard code if you promote the guard into a Dynamo guard (since that\n        # allows for the guard to be done using C++ guards.)  If we get\n        # ShapeEnv guards to go into C++ guards, this will stop being a thing\n        # though!\n\n        assert self.should_exit\n\n        # Miniature DCE pass, but only for obviously trivial operations\n        def is_static_true(b_node: fx.node.Argument) -> bool:\n            if b_node is True:\n                return True\n            if not isinstance(b_node, fx.Node):\n                return False\n            b = b_node.meta.get(\"example_value\")\n            if b is None:\n                return False\n            if b is True:\n                return True\n            if (\n                isinstance(b, torch.SymBool)\n                and (r := b.node.maybe_as_bool()) is not None\n            ):\n                return r\n            # TODO: We can also technically remove all cases when the input\n            # doesn't have unbacked inputs, since it's all in the ShapeEnv\n            return False\n\n        def is_symnode_arg(a: fx.node.Argument) -> bool:\n            from torch.fx.experimental.sym_node import SymTypes\n\n            if isinstance(a, (int, float, bool)):\n                return True\n            if isinstance(a, fx.Node):\n                return isinstance(a.meta.get(\"example_value\"), SymTypes)\n            return False\n\n        # NB: We assume that you cannot do mutations on int/float/bool,\n        # because they are immutable types, and therefore is always safe to\n        # DCE.\n        def is_symnode_compute_node(node: fx.Node) -> bool:\n            from torch.fx.experimental.sym_node import SymTypes\n\n            if node.op != \"call_function\":\n                return False\n            # TODO: I don't think it's possible to have a bare int/float here?\n            if not isinstance(node.meta.get(\"example_value\"), SymTypes):\n                return False\n            # TODO: This will bail here if you ever end up with a more complicated\n            # computation function, like sum(list_of_ints), even though it\n            # should be DCE'able\n            if not all(is_symnode_arg(a) for a in node.args):\n                return False\n            if not all(is_symnode_arg(a) for a in node.kwargs.values()):\n                return False\n            return True\n\n        from torch.fx.experimental.symbolic_shapes import is_accessor_node\n\n        for node in reversed(list(self.graph.nodes)):\n            if len(list(node.users)) == 0:\n                if (\n                    node.op == \"get_attr\"\n                    or (node.op == \"call_function\" and node.target is operator.getitem)\n                    or (\n                        node.op == \"call_function\"\n                        and node.target is torch._check\n                        and is_static_true(node.args[0])\n                    )\n                    or is_symnode_compute_node(node)\n                    or is_accessor_node(node)\n                ):\n                    self.remove_node(node)\n\n        def placeholder_binds_symbol(node: fx.Node) -> Optional[sympy.Symbol]:\n            arg = node.meta[\"grapharg\"]\n            example = arg.example\n            if isinstance(example, torch.SymInt) and isinstance(\n                example.node.expr, sympy.Symbol\n            ):\n                return example.node.expr\n            return None\n\n        def remove_unused(node: fx.Node) -> None:\n            log.debug(\"REMOVE UNUSED GRAPHARG %s\", node.meta[\"grapharg\"].source.name())\n            # I'm not really sure why you need to delete these from the\n            # node since the node is going to get removed\n            del node.meta[\"grapharg\"]\n            self.remove_node(node)\n            self.real_value_cache.pop(node, None)\n\n        used_symbols: set[sympy.Symbol] = set()\n\n        def update_used_symbols(\n            used_symbols: set[sympy.Symbol], fake: Union[torch.SymInt, torch.Tensor]\n        ) -> None:\n            used_symbols |= free_symbols(fake)\n\n        recheck_placeholders = []\n        for node in self.placeholders:\n            binds_symbol = placeholder_binds_symbol(node) is not None\n            # Don't delete symbol bindings yet\n            if binds_symbol:\n                if not node.users:\n                    recheck_placeholders.append(node)\n            else:\n                if not node.users and not isinstance(\n                    node.meta[\"grapharg\"], BackwardStateGraphArg\n                ):\n                    remove_unused(node)\n                else:\n                    # Register the free symbols as uses\n                    arg = node.meta[\"grapharg\"]\n                    if isinstance(arg, BackwardStateGraphArg):\n                        continue\n                    if isinstance(node.meta[\"grapharg\"].example, torch.ScriptObject):\n                        real_script_obj = node.meta[\"grapharg\"].example\n                        fake_script_obj = node.meta[\"grapharg\"].example_strong_ref\n                        if not torch._library.fake_class_registry.tracing_with_real(\n                            real_script_obj\n                        ):\n                            flat_dict = dict(real_script_obj.__obj_flatten__())  # type: ignore[attr-defined]\n                            for attr in flat_dict.keys():\n                                fake_attr_val = getattr(\n                                    fake_script_obj.wrapped_obj, attr\n                                )\n                                pytree.tree_map_only(\n                                    (torch.SymInt, torch.Tensor),\n                                    lambda t: update_used_symbols(used_symbols, t),\n                                    fake_attr_val,\n                                )\n                        continue\n                    fake = (\n                        arg.fake_tensor if arg.fake_tensor is not None else arg.example\n                    )\n                    update_used_symbols(used_symbols, fake)\n\n        # After removing unused graphargs, prune unused binds_symbol\n        for node in recheck_placeholders:\n            symbol = placeholder_binds_symbol(node)\n            if symbol is not None:\n                if symbol not in used_symbols:\n                    remove_unused(node)\n                else:\n                    # Make sure we delete later occurrences of the same symbol\n                    used_symbols.remove(symbol)\n\n    def remove_tensorify_specialized_graphargs(self) -> None:\n        # This is a pretty interesting function. Basically we have this problem\n        # where our compiler tends to choke when we have unused inputs. The way\n        # we support dynamic float arguments is by doing a joint fx pass and\n        # tensorifying away as many symfloats as we can. For the remaining symfloats\n        # we have no choice but to specialize... HOWEVER at that point in time\n        # we can no longer remove graph inputs. So our sledgehammer solution is to\n        # save the state of what inputs we should have specialized in dynamo and\n        # restart analysis. This function incorporates this \"view from the future\"\n        # state and specializes inputs that we know we won't be able to tensorify\n        # away in the joint pass. In principle we shouldn't choke on unused inputs\n        # and so this shouldn't be necessary. In practice CUDA graphs choke on\n        # unused inputs so we need this for now.\n\n        # Import here to prevent circular import\n        from torch._dynamo.symbolic_convert import TensorifyState\n\n        for node in self.graph.nodes:\n            example_value = node.meta.get(\"example_value\")\n            if (\n                isinstance(example_value, FakeTensor)\n                and example_value.item_memo is not None\n                and hasattr(example_value.item_memo.node._expr, \"name\")\n                and all(u.target == \"item\" for u in node.users)\n                and TensorifyState.should_specialize(\n                    # We use _expr instead of expr b/c we want the symbol not the replacement\n                    example_value.item_memo.node._expr.name\n                )\n            ):\n                for u in list(node.users):\n                    u.replace_all_uses_with(guard_scalar(example_value.item_memo))\n                    self.remove_node(u)\n                self.remove_node(node)\n\n    def add_output_instructions(self, prefix: list[Instruction]) -> None:\n        \"\"\"\n        We call this on the creation of a new compiled subgraph that is inserted\n        before user code.\n        \"\"\"\n        self.output_instructions.extend(prefix)\n        self.should_exit = True\n\n    def install_global_unsafe(self, name: str, value: Any) -> None:\n        \"\"\"\n        WARNING: prefer the safer `install_global_by_id/install_global`.\n        torch.compile instances should be independent of each other;\n        one footgun is to have one instance depend on the existence of\n        a global installed by another instance. This can happen if we mangle\n        a global the same way across both instances.\n        \"\"\"\n        assert name not in self.installed_globals\n        self.installed_globals.add(name)\n        self.cleanups.append(CleanupHook.create(self.global_scope, name, value))\n\n    def install_global_by_id(self, prefix: str, value: Any) -> str:\n        \"\"\"\n        Installs a global if it hasn't been installed already.\n        This is determined by (prefix, id(value)) pair.\n\n        Returns the name of the newly installed global.\n        \"\"\"\n        # NB: unique_id is unique, even across torch.compile instances\n        name = unique_id(prefix)\n        self.install_global_unsafe(name, value)\n        return name\n\n    def cleanup(self) -> None:\n        # There is a reference cycle between tracer and OutputGraph, causing\n        # some of the tensor objects to be held alive for longer than necessary.\n        self.root_tx = None  # type: ignore[assignment]\n        self.nn_modules.clear()\n        self.param_name_to_source = None\n\n        for node in self.graph.nodes:\n            if \"grapharg\" in node.meta:\n                del node.meta[\"grapharg\"]\n        self.real_value_cache.clear()\n        self.input_name_to_proxy.clear()\n        self.side_effects.clear()\n        self.variable_tracker_cache.clear()\n        self.register_finalizer_fns.clear()\n        self.dynamo_flat_name_to_original_fqn.clear()\n        self.tracing_context.clear()\n        self.input_source_to_var.clear()\n        self.unspec_variable_map.clear()\n        self.backward_state.clear()\n\n    def cleanup_failed_trace(self) -> None:\n        \"\"\"\n        Clean up reference cycles after a failed trace.\n\n        The nodes of the graph form a doubly linked list and point back to the\n        graph, creating a reference cycle. Additionally, FakeTensorMode ->\n        ShapeEnv -> TrackedFake -> FakeTensor -> FakeTensorMode creates a cycle.\n\n        Manually cleaning up these cycles allows GC without a full gc pass.\n        \"\"\"\n        # Break the graph nodes' doubly linked list cycle so they can be\n        # immediately collected by reference counting.\n        for node in list(self.graph.nodes):\n            node._prev = node._next = None\n        # Clear ShapeEnv -> TrackedFake -> FakeTensor -> FakeTensorMode cycle.\n        self.tracked_fakes.clear()\n\n    def add_graph_finalizer(\n        self, register_finalizer: Callable[[fx.GraphModule], None]\n    ) -> None:\n        self.register_finalizer_fns.append(register_finalizer)\n\n    def example_value_from_input_node(self, node: torch.fx.Node)
